header.title: The Body of Christ
header.description: These are references where it says the church is the body of Christ

blocks.title: Released From Law, Bound to Christ

texts.NIV84: Do you not know, brothers--for I am speaking to men who know the law--that the law has authority over a man only as long as he lives?
texts.KJV: Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?
texts.NASB95: Or do you not know, brethren (for I am speaking to those who know the law), that the law has jurisdiction over a person as long as he lives?
texts.NKJV: Or do you not know, brethren (for I speak to those who know the law), that the law has dominion over a man as long as he lives?
texts.DRB: KNOW you not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) that the law hath dominion over a man, as long as it liveth?
texts.ESV: Or do you not know, brothers—for I am speaking to those who know the law—that the law is binding on a person only as long as he lives?
texts.LEB: Or do you not know, brothers (for I am speaking to those who know the law), that the law is master of a person for as long a time as he lives?
texts.AMP: Or do you not know, brothers and sisters (for I am speaking to those who know the Law), that the Law has jurisdiction [to rule] over a person as long as he lives?
texts.NLT: Now, dear brothers and sisters—you who are familiar with the law—don’t you know that the law applies only while a person is living?
texts.AKJV: Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?
texts.NRSV: Or do you not know, brothers and sisters—for I am speaking to those who know the law—that the law is binding on a person only during that person’s lifetime?

texts.NIV84: For example, by law a married woman is bound to her husband as long as he is alive, but if her husband dies, she is released from the law of marriage.
texts.KJV: For the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
texts.NASB95: For the married woman is bound by law to her husband while he is living; but if her husband dies, she is released from the law concerning the husband.
texts.NKJV: For the woman who has a husband is bound by the law to her husband as long as he lives. But if the husband dies, she is released from the law of her husband.
texts.DRB: For the woman that hath an husband, whilst her husband liveth is bound to the law. But if her husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
texts.ESV: For a married woman is bound by law to her husband while he lives, but if her husband dies she is released from the law of marriage.
texts.LEB: For the married woman is bound by law to her husband while he lives, but if her husband dies, she is released from the law of the husband.
texts.AMP: For the married woman [as an example] is bound and remains bound by law to her husband while he lives; but if her husband dies, she is released and exempt from the law concerning her husband.
texts.NLT: For example, when a woman marries, the law binds her to her husband as long as he is alive. But if he dies, the laws of marriage no longer apply to her.
texts.AKJV: For the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
texts.NRSV: Thus a married woman is bound by the law to her husband as long as he lives, but if her husband dies, she is discharged from the law concerning the husband.

texts.NIV84: So then, if she marries another man while her husband is still alive, she is called an adulteress. But if her husband dies, she is released from that law and is not an adulteress, even though she marries another man.
texts.KJV: So then if, while her husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.
texts.NASB95: So then, if while her husband is living she is joined to another man, she shall be called an adulteress; but if her husband dies, she is free from the law, so that she is not an adulteress though she is joined to another man.
texts.NKJV: So then if, while her husband lives, she marries another man, she will be called an adulteress; but if her husband dies, she is free from that law, so that she is no adulteress, though she has married another man.
texts.DRB: Therefore, whilst her husband liveth, she shall be called an adulteress, if she be with another man: but if her husband be dead, she is delivered from the law of her husband; so that she is not an adulteress, if she be with another man.
texts.ESV: Accordingly, she will be called an adulteress if she lives with another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law, and if she marries another man she is not an adulteress.
texts.LEB: Therefore as a result, if she belongs to another man while her husband is living, she will be called an adulteress. But if her husband dies, she is free from the law, so that she is not an adulteress if she belongs to another man.
texts.AMP: Accordingly, she will be designated as an adulteress if she unites herself to another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from the law [regarding marriage], so that she is not an adulteress if she marries another man.
texts.NLT: So while her husband is alive, she would be committing adultery if she married another man. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law and does not commit adultery when she remarries.
texts.AKJV: So then if, while her husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.
texts.NRSV: Accordingly, she will be called an adulteress if she belongs to another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law, and if she belongs to another man, she is not an adulteress.

texts.NIV84: So, my brothers, you also died to the law through the body of Christ, that you might belong to another, to him who was raised from the dead, in order that we might bear fruit to God.
texts.KJV: Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become dead to the law by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, even to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, my brethren, you also were made to die to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you might be joined to another, to Him who was raised from the dead, in order that we might bear fruit for God.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, my brethren, you also have become dead to the law through the body of Christ, that you may be married to another—to Him who was raised from the dead, that we should bear fruit to God.
texts.DRB: Therefore, my brethren, you also are become dead to the law, by the body of Christ; that you may belong to another, who is risen again from the dead, that we may bring forth fruit to God.
texts.ESV: Likewise, my brothers, you also have died to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to him who has been raised from the dead, in order that we may bear fruit for God.
texts.LEB: So then, my brothers, you also were brought to death with respect to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to the one who was raised from the dead, in order that we may bear fruit for God.
texts.AMP: Therefore, my fellow believers, you too died to the Law through the [crucified] body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to Him who was raised from the dead, in order that we may bear fruit for God.
texts.NLT: So, my dear brothers and sisters, this is the point: You died to the power of the law when you died with Christ. And now you are united with the one who was raised from the dead. As a result, we can produce a harvest of good deeds for God.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become dead to the law by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, even to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.
texts.NRSV: In the same way, my brothers and sisters, you have died to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to him who was raised from the dead in order that we may bear fruit for God.

texts.NIV84: For when we were controlled by the sinful nature, the sinful passions aroused by the law were at work in our bodies, so that we bore fruit for death.
texts.KJV: For when we were in the flesh, the motions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto death.
texts.NASB95: For while we were in the flesh, the sinful passions, which were aroused by the Law, were at work in the members of our body to bear fruit for death.
texts.NKJV: For when we were in the flesh, the sinful passions which were aroused by the law were at work in our members to bear fruit to death.
texts.DRB: For when we were in the flesh, the passions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members, to bring forth fruit unto death.
texts.ESV: For while we were living in the flesh, our sinful passions, aroused by the law, were at work in our members to bear fruit for death.
texts.LEB: For when we were in the flesh, sinful desires were working through the law in our members, to bear fruit for death.
texts.AMP: When we were living in the flesh [trapped by sin], the sinful passions, which were awakened by [that which] the Law [identifies as sin], were at work in our body to bear fruit for death [since the willingness to sin led to death and separation from God].
texts.NLT: When we were controlled by our old nature, sinful desires were at work within us, and the law aroused these evil desires that produced a harvest of sinful deeds, resulting in death.
texts.AKJV: For when we were in the flesh, the motions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto death.
texts.NRSV: For while we were living in the flesh, our sinful passions, aroused by the law, were at work in our members to bear fruit for death.

texts.NIV84: But now, by dying to what once bound us, we have been released from the law so that we serve in the new way of the Spirit, and not in the old way of the written code.
texts.KJV: But now we are delivered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
texts.NASB95: But now we have been released from the Law, having died to that by which we were bound, so that we serve in newness of the Spirit and not in oldness of the letter.
texts.NKJV: But now we have been delivered from the law, having died to what we were held by, so that we should serve in the newness of the Spirit and not in the oldness of the letter.
texts.DRB: But now we are loosed from the law of death, wherein we were detained; so that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
texts.ESV: But now we are released from the law, having died to that which held us captive, so that we serve in the new way of the Spirit and not in the old way of the written code.
texts.LEB: But now we have been released from the law, because we have died to that by which we were bound, so that we may serve in newness of the Spirit and not in oldness of the letter of the law.
texts.AMP: But now we have been released from the Law and its penalty, having died [through Christ] to that by which we were held captive, so that we serve [God] in the newness of the Spirit and not in the oldness of the letter [of the Law].
texts.NLT: But now we have been released from the law, for we died to it and are no longer captive to its power. Now we can serve God, not in the old way of obeying the letter of the law, but in the new way of living in the Spirit.
texts.AKJV: But now we are delivered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
texts.NRSV: But now we are discharged from the law, dead to that which held us captive, so that we are enslaved in the newness of the Spirit and not in the oldness of the written code.

texts.NIV84: Therefore, there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus,
texts.KJV: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: Therefore there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
texts.NKJV: There is therefore now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus, who do not walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit.
texts.DRB: THERE is now therefore no condemnation to them that are in Christ Jesus, who walk not according to the flesh.
texts.ESV: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
texts.LEB: Consequently, there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
texts.AMP: Therefore there is now no condemnation [no guilty verdict, no punishment] for those who are in Christ Jesus [who believe in Him as personal Lord and Savior].
texts.NLT: So now there is no condemnation for those who belong to Christ Jesus.
texts.AKJV: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
texts.NRSV: Therefore there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

texts.NIV84: because through Christ Jesus the law of the Spirit of life set me free from the law of sin and death.
texts.KJV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
texts.NASB95: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
texts.NKJV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has made me free from the law of sin and death.
texts.DRB: For the law of the spirit of life, in Christ Jesus, hath delivered me from the law of sin and of death.
texts.ESV: For the law of the Spirit of life has set you free in Christ Jesus from the law of sin and death.
texts.LEB: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
texts.AMP: For the law of the Spirit of life [which is] in Christ Jesus [the law of our new being] has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
texts.NLT: And because you belong to him, the power of the life-giving Spirit has freed you from the power of sin that leads to death.
texts.AKJV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
texts.NRSV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.

texts.NIV84: For what the law was powerless to do in that it was weakened by the sinful nature, God did by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful man to be a sin offering. And so he condemned sin in sinful man,
texts.KJV: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
texts.NASB95: For what the Law could not do, weak as it was through the flesh, God did: sending His own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and as an offering for sin, He condemned sin in the flesh,
texts.NKJV: For what the law could not do in that it was weak through the flesh, God did by sending His own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, on account of sin: He condemned sin in the flesh,
texts.DRB: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh; God sending his own Son, in the likeness of sinful flesh and of sin, hath condemned sin in the flesh;
texts.ESV: For God has done what the law, weakened by the flesh, could not do. By sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,
texts.LEB: For what was impossible for the law, in that it was weak through the flesh, God did. By sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and concerning sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,
texts.AMP: For what the Law could not do [that is, overcome sin and remove its penalty, its power] being weakened by the flesh [man’s nature without the Holy Spirit], God did: He sent His own Son in the likeness of sinful man as an offering for sin. And He condemned sin in the flesh [subdued it and overcame it in the person of His own Son],
texts.NLT: The law of Moses was unable to save us because of the weakness of our sinful nature. So God did what the law could not do. He sent his own Son in a body like the bodies we sinners have. And in that body God declared an end to sin’s control over us by giving his Son as a sacrifice for our sins.
texts.AKJV: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
texts.NRSV: For God has done what the law, weakened by the flesh, could not do: by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and to deal with sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,

texts.NIV84: in order that the righteous requirements of the law might be fully met in us, who do not live according to the sinful nature but according to the Spirit.
texts.KJV: That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: so that the requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us, who do not walk according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
texts.NKJV: that the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us who do not walk according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
texts.DRB: That the justification of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh, but according to the spirit.
texts.ESV: in order that the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
texts.LEB: in order that the requirement of the law would be fulfilled in us, who do not live according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
texts.AMP: so that the [righteous and just] requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us who do not live our lives in the ways of the flesh [guided by worldliness and our sinful nature], but [live our lives] in the ways of the Spirit [guided by His power].
texts.NLT: He did this so that the just requirement of the law would be fully satisfied for us, who no longer follow our sinful nature but instead follow the Spirit.
texts.AKJV: that the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
texts.NRSV: so that the just requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.

texts.NIV84: Those who live according to the sinful nature have their minds set on what that nature desires; but those who live in accordance with the Spirit have their minds set on what the Spirit desires.
texts.KJV: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: For those who are according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who are according to the Spirit, the things of the Spirit.
texts.NKJV: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit, the things of the Spirit.
texts.DRB: For they that are according to the flesh, mind the things that are of the flesh; but they that are according to the spirit, mind the things that are of the spirit.
texts.ESV: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit.
texts.LEB: For those who are living according to the flesh are intent on the things of the flesh, but those who are living according to the Spirit are intent on the things of the Spirit.
texts.AMP: For those who are living according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh [which gratify the body], but those who are living according to the Spirit, [set their minds on] the things of the Spirit [His will and purpose].
texts.NLT: Those who are dominated by the sinful nature think about sinful things, but those who are controlled by the Holy Spirit think about things that please the Spirit.
texts.AKJV: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
texts.NRSV: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit.

texts.NIV84: The mind of sinful man is death, but the mind controlled by the Spirit is life and peace;
texts.KJV: For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
texts.NASB95: For the mind set on the flesh is death, but the mind set on the Spirit is life and peace,
texts.NKJV: For to be carnally minded is death, but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
texts.DRB: For the wisdom of the flesh is death; but the wisdom of the spirit is life and peace.
texts.ESV: For to set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace.
texts.LEB: For the mindset of the flesh is death, but the mindset of the Spirit is life and peace,
texts.AMP: Now the mind of the flesh is death [both now and forever—because it pursues sin]; but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace [the spiritual well-being that comes from walking with God—both now and forever];
texts.NLT: So letting your sinful nature control your mind leads to death. But letting the Spirit control your mind leads to life and peace.
texts.AKJV: For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
texts.NRSV: To set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace.

texts.NIV84: the sinful mind is hostile to God. It does not submit to God's law, nor can it do so.
texts.KJV: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
texts.NASB95: because the mind set on the flesh is hostile toward God; for it does not subject itself to the law of God, for it is not even able to do so,
texts.NKJV: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God; for it is not subject to the law of God, nor indeed can be.
texts.DRB: Because the wisdom of the flesh is an enemy to God; for it is not subject to the law of God, neither can it be.
texts.ESV: For the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God, for it does not submit to God's law; indeed, it cannot.
texts.LEB: because the mindset of the flesh is enmity toward God, for it is not subjected to the law of God, for it is not able to do so,
texts.AMP: the mind of the flesh [with its sinful pursuits] is actively hostile to God. It does not submit itself to God’s law, since it cannot,
texts.NLT: For the sinful nature is always hostile to God. It never did obey God’s laws, and it never will.
texts.AKJV: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
texts.NRSV: For this reason the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God; it does not submit to God’s law—indeed, it cannot,

texts.NIV84: Those controlled by the sinful nature cannot please God.
texts.KJV: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
texts.NASB95: and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
texts.NKJV: So then, those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
texts.DRB: And they who are in the flesh, cannot please God.
texts.ESV: Those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
texts.LEB: and those who are in the flesh are not able to please God.
texts.AMP: and those who are in the flesh [living a life that caters to sinful appetites and impulses] cannot please God.
texts.NLT: That’s why those who are still under the control of their sinful nature can never please God.
texts.AKJV: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
texts.NRSV: and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.

texts.NIV84: You, however, are controlled not by the sinful nature but by the Spirit, if the Spirit of God lives in you. And if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, he does not belong to Christ.
texts.KJV: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
texts.NASB95: However, you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God dwells in you. But if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, he does not belong to Him.
texts.NKJV: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God dwells in you. Now if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, he is not His.
texts.DRB: But you are not in the flesh, but in the spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
texts.ESV: You, however, are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if in fact the Spirit of God dwells in you. Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.
texts.LEB: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God lives in you. But if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, this person does not belong to him.
texts.AMP: However, you are not [living] in the flesh [controlled by the sinful nature] but in the Spirit, if in fact the Spirit of God lives in you [directing and guiding you]. But if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, he does not belong to Him [and is not a child of God].
texts.NLT: But you are not controlled by your sinful nature. You are controlled by the Spirit if you have the Spirit of God living in you. (And remember that those who do not have the Spirit of Christ living in them do not belong to him at all.)
texts.AKJV: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
texts.NRSV: But you are not in the flesh; you are in the Spirit, since the Spirit of God dwells in you. Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.

texts.NIV84: But if Christ is in you, your body is dead because of sin, yet your spirit is alive because of righteousness.
texts.KJV: And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
texts.NASB95: If Christ is in you, though the body is dead because of sin, yet the spirit is alive because of righteousness.
texts.NKJV: And if Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
texts.DRB: And if Christ be in you, the body indeed is dead, because of sin; but the spirit liveth, because of justification.
texts.ESV: But if Christ is in you, although the body is dead because of sin, the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
texts.LEB: But if Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
texts.AMP: If Christ lives in you, though your [natural] body is dead because of sin, your spirit is alive because of righteousness [which He provides].
texts.NLT: And Christ lives within you, so even though your body will die because of sin, the Spirit gives you life because you have been made right with God.
texts.AKJV: And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
texts.NRSV: But if Christ is in you, then the body is dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.

texts.NIV84: And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit, who lives in you.
texts.KJV: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
texts.NASB95: But if the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, He who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through His Spirit who dwells in you.
texts.NKJV: But if the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, He who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through His Spirit who dwells in you.
texts.DRB: And if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead, dwell in you; he that raised up Jesus Christ from the dead, shall quicken also your mortal bodies, because of his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
texts.ESV: If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit who dwells in you.
texts.LEB: And if the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also make alive your mortal bodies through his Spirit who lives in you.
texts.AMP: And if the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, He who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through His Spirit, who lives in you.
texts.NLT: The Spirit of God, who raised Jesus from the dead, lives in you. And just as God raised Christ Jesus from the dead, he will give life to your mortal bodies by this same Spirit living within you.
texts.AKJV: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
texts.NRSV: If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will give life to your mortal bodies also through his Spirit that dwells in you.

texts.NIV84: Therefore, brothers, we have an obligation--but it is not to the sinful nature, to live according to it.
texts.KJV: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
texts.NASB95: So then, brethren, we are under obligation, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh—
texts.NKJV: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors—not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
texts.DRB: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
texts.ESV: So then, brothers, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
texts.LEB: So then, brothers, we are obligated not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
texts.AMP: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but not to our flesh [our human nature, our worldliness, our sinful capacity], to live according to the [impulses of the] flesh [our nature without the Holy Spirit]—
texts.NLT: Therefore, dear brothers and sisters, you have no obligation to do what your sinful nature urges you to do.
texts.AKJV: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
texts.NRSV: So then, brothers and sisters, we are obligated, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh—

texts.NIV84: For if you live according to the sinful nature, you will die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the misdeeds of the body, you will live,
texts.KJV: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
texts.NASB95: for if you are living according to the flesh, you must die; but if by the Spirit you are putting to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
texts.NKJV: For if you live according to the flesh you will die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
texts.DRB: For if you live according to the flesh, you shall die: but if by the Spirit you mortify the deeds of the flesh, you shall live.
texts.ESV: For if you live according to the flesh you will die, but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
texts.LEB: For if you live according to the flesh, you are going to die, but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
texts.AMP: for if you are living according to the [impulses of the] flesh, you are going to die. But if [you are living] by the [power of the Holy] Spirit you are habitually putting to death the sinful deeds of the body, you will [really] live forever .
texts.NLT: For if you live by its dictates, you will die. But if through the power of the Spirit you put to death the deeds of your sinful nature, you will live.
texts.AKJV: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
texts.NRSV: for if you live according to the flesh, you will die, but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.

texts.NIV84: because those who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
texts.KJV: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
texts.NASB95: For all who are being led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.
texts.NKJV: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.
texts.DRB: For whosoever are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
texts.ESV: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
texts.LEB: For all those who are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.
texts.AMP: For all who are allowing themselves to be led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
texts.NLT: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.
texts.AKJV: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
texts.NRSV: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.

texts.NIV84: For you did not receive a spirit that makes you a slave again to fear, but you received the Spirit of sonship. And by him we cry, "Abba, Father."
texts.KJV: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
texts.NASB95: For you have not received a spirit of slavery leading to fear again, but you have received a spirit of adoption as sons by which we cry out, “Abba! Father!”
texts.NKJV: For you did not receive the spirit of bondage again to fear, but you received the Spirit of adoption by whom we cry out, “Abba, Father.”
texts.DRB: For you have not received the spirit of bondage again in fear; but you have received the spirit of adoption of sons, whereby we cry: Abba (Father).
texts.ESV: For you did not receive the spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, “Abba! Father!”
texts.LEB: For you have not received a spirit of slavery leading to fear again, but you have received the Spirit of adoption, by whom we cry out, “Abba! Father!”
texts.AMP: For you have not received a spirit of slavery leading again to fear [of God’s judgment], but you have received the Spirit of adoption as sons [the Spirit producing sonship] by which we [joyfully] cry, “Abba! Father!”
texts.NLT: So you have not received a spirit that makes you fearful slaves. Instead, you received God’s Spirit when he adopted you as his own children. Now we call him, “Abba, Father.”
texts.AKJV: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
texts.NRSV: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you received a spirit of adoption. When we cry, “Abba! Father!”

texts.NIV84: The Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are God's children.
texts.KJV: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
texts.NASB95: The Spirit Himself testifies with our spirit that we are children of God,
texts.NKJV: The Spirit Himself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God,
texts.DRB: For the Spirit himself giveth testimony to our spirit, that we are the sons of God.
texts.ESV: The Spirit himself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God,
texts.LEB: The Spirit himself confirms to our spirit that we are children of God,
texts.AMP: The Spirit Himself testifies and confirms together with our spirit [assuring us] that we [believers] are children of God.
texts.NLT: For his Spirit joins with our spirit to affirm that we are God’s children.
texts.AKJV: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
texts.NRSV: it is that very Spirit bearing witness with our spirit that we are children of God,

texts.NIV84: Now if we are children, then we are heirs--heirs of God and co-heirs with Christ, if indeed we share in his sufferings in order that we may also share in his glory.
texts.KJV: And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
texts.NASB95: and if children, heirs also, heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with Him so that we may also be glorified with Him.
texts.NKJV: and if children, then heirs—heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with Him, that we may also be glorified together.
texts.DRB: And if sons, heirs also; heirs indeed of God, and joint heirs with Christ: yet so, if we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified with him.
texts.ESV: and if children, then heirs—heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, provided we suffer with him in order that we may also be glorified with him.
texts.LEB: and if children, also heirs—heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer together with him so that we may also be glorified together with him.
texts.AMP: And if [we are His] children, [then we are His] heirs also: heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ [sharing His spiritual blessing and inheritance], if indeed we share in His suffering so that we may also share in His glory.
texts.NLT: And since we are his children, we are his heirs. In fact, together with Christ we are heirs of God’s glory. But if we are to share his glory, we must also share his suffering.
texts.AKJV: and if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
texts.NRSV: and if children, then heirs: heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if we in fact suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him.

blocks.title: Service in the Body of Christ

texts.NIV84: Therefore, I urge you, brothers, in view of God's mercy, to offer your bodies as living sacrifices, holy and pleasing to God--this is your spiritual act of worship.
texts.KJV: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
texts.NASB95: Therefore I urge you, brethren, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living and holy sacrifice, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service of worship.
texts.NKJV: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that you present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your reasonable service.
texts.DRB: I BESEECH you therefore, brethren, by the mercy of God, that you present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, pleasing unto God, your reasonable service.
texts.ESV: I appeal to you therefore, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
texts.LEB: Therefore I exhort you, brothers, through the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God, which is your reasonable service.
texts.AMP: Therefore I urge you, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies [dedicating all of yourselves, set apart] as a living sacrifice, holy and well-pleasing to God, which is your rational (logical, intelligent) act of worship.
texts.NLT: And so, dear brothers and sisters, I plead with you to give your bodies to God because of all he has done for you. Let them be a living and holy sacrifice—the kind he will find acceptable. This is truly the way to worship him.
texts.AKJV: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
texts.NRSV: I appeal to you therefore, brothers and sisters, on the basis of God’s mercy, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your reasonable act of worship.

texts.NIV84: Do not conform any longer to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God's will is--his good, pleasing and perfect will.
texts.KJV: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
texts.NASB95: And do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what the will of God is, that which is good and acceptable and perfect.
texts.NKJV: And do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, that you may prove what is that good and acceptable and perfect will of God.
texts.DRB: And be not conformed to this world; but be reformed in the newness of your mind, that you may prove what is the good, and the acceptable, and the perfect will of God.
texts.ESV: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and acceptable and perfect.
texts.LEB: And do not be conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, so that you may approve what is the good and well-pleasing and perfect will of God.
texts.AMP: And do not be conformed to this world [any longer with its superficial values and customs], but be transformed and progressively changed [as you mature spiritually] by the renewing of your mind [focusing on godly values and ethical attitudes], so that you may prove [for yourselves] what the will of God is, that which is good and acceptable and perfect [in His plan and purpose for you].
texts.NLT: Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect.
texts.AKJV: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
texts.NRSV: Do not be conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewing of the mind, so that you may discern what is the will of God—what is good and acceptable and perfect.

texts.NIV84: For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but rather think of yourself with sober judgment, in accordance with the measure of faith God has given you.
texts.KJV: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
texts.NASB95: For through the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think more highly of himself than he ought to think; but to think so as to have sound judgment, as God has allotted to each a measure of faith.
texts.NKJV: For I say, through the grace given to me, to everyone who is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think soberly, as God has dealt to each one a measure of faith.
texts.DRB: For I say, by the grace that is given me, to all that are among you, not to be more wise than it behoveth to be wise, but to be wise unto sobriety, and according as God hath divided to every one the measure of faith.
texts.ESV: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.
texts.LEB: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone who is among you not to think more highly of yourself than what one ought to think, but to think sensibly, as God has apportioned a measure of faith to each one.
texts.AMP: For by the grace [of God] given to me I say to everyone of you not to think more highly of himself [and of his importance and ability] than he ought to think; but to think so as to have sound judgment, as God has apportioned to each a degree of faith [and a purpose designed for service].
texts.NLT: Because of the privilege and authority God has given me, I give each of you this warning: Don’t think you are better than you really are. Be honest in your evaluation of yourselves, measuring yourselves by the faith God has given us.
texts.AKJV: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
texts.NRSV: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of yourself more highly than you ought to think but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.

texts.NIV84: Just as each of us has one body with many members, and these members do not all have the same function,
texts.KJV: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:
texts.NASB95: For just as we have many members in one body and all the members do not have the same function,
texts.NKJV: For as we have many members in one body, but all the members do not have the same function,
texts.DRB: For as in one body we have many members, but all the members have not the same office:
texts.ESV: For as in one body we have many members, and the members do not all have the same function,
texts.LEB: For just as in one body we have many members, but all the members do not have the same function,
texts.AMP: For just as in one [physical] body we have many parts, and these parts do not all have the same function or special use,
texts.NLT: Just as our bodies have many parts and each part has a special function,
texts.AKJV: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:
texts.NRSV: For as in one body we have many members and not all the members have the same function,

texts.NIV84: so in Christ we who are many form one body, and each member belongs to all the others.
texts.KJV: So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
texts.NASB95: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
texts.NKJV: so we, being many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another.
texts.DRB: So we being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
texts.ESV: so we, though many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
texts.LEB: in the same way we who are many are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another,
texts.AMP: so we, who are many, are [nevertheless just] one body in Christ, and individually [we are] parts one of another [mutually dependent on each other].
texts.NLT: so it is with Christ’s body. We are many parts of one body, and we all belong to each other.
texts.AKJV: so we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
texts.NRSV: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually we are members one of another.

texts.NIV84: We have different gifts, according to the grace given us. If a man's gift is prophesying, let him use it in proportion to his faith.
texts.KJV: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith;
texts.NASB95: Since we have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, each of us is to exercise them accordingly: if prophecy, according to the proportion of his faith;
texts.NKJV: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, let us prophesy in proportion to our faith;
texts.DRB: And having different gifts, according to the grace that is given us, either prophecy, to be used according to the rule of faith;
texts.ESV: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, in proportion to our faith;
texts.LEB: but having different gifts according to the grace given to us: if it is prophecy, according to the proportion of his faith;
texts.AMP: Since we have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, each of us is to use them accordingly: if [someone has the gift of] prophecy, [let him speak a new message from God to His people] in proportion to the faith possessed;
texts.NLT: In his grace, God has given us different gifts for doing certain things well. So if God has given you the ability to prophesy, speak out with as much faith as God has given you.
texts.AKJV: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith;
texts.NRSV: We have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us: prophecy, in proportion to faith;

texts.NIV84: If it is serving, let him serve; if it is teaching, let him teach;
texts.KJV: Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
texts.NASB95: if service, in his serving; or he who teaches, in his teaching;
texts.NKJV: or ministry, let us use it in our ministering; he who teaches, in teaching;
texts.DRB: Or ministry, in ministering; or he that teacheth, in doctrine;
texts.ESV: if service, in our serving; the one who teaches, in his teaching;
texts.LEB: if it is service, by service; if it is one who teaches, by teaching;
texts.AMP: if service, in the act of serving; or he who teaches, in the act of teaching;
texts.NLT: If your gift is serving others, serve them well. If you are a teacher, teach well.
texts.AKJV: or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
texts.NRSV: ministry, in ministering; the teacher, in teaching;

texts.NIV84: if it is encouraging, let him encourage; if it is contributing to the needs of others, let him give generously; if it is leadership, let him govern diligently; if it is showing mercy, let him do it cheerfully.
texts.KJV: Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.NASB95: or he who exhorts, in his exhortation; he who gives, with liberality; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.NKJV: he who exhorts, in exhortation; he who gives, with liberality; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.DRB: He that exhorteth, in exhorting; he that giveth, with simplicity; he that ruleth, with carefulness; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.ESV: the one who exhorts, in his exhortation; the one who contributes, in generosity; the one who leads, with zeal; the one who does acts of mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.LEB: if it is one who exhorts, by exhortation; one who gives, with sincerity; one who leads, with diligence; one who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.AMP: or he who encourages, in the act of encouragement; he who gives, with generosity; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy [in caring for others], with cheerfulness.
texts.NLT: If your gift is to encourage others, be encouraging. If it is giving, give generously. If God has given you leadership ability, take the responsibility seriously. And if you have a gift for showing kindness to others, do it gladly.
texts.AKJV: or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
texts.NRSV: the encourager, in encouragement; the giver, in sincerity; the leader, in diligence; the compassionate, in cheerfulness.

texts.NIV84: The law is only a shadow of the good things that are coming--not the realities themselves. For this reason it can never, by the same sacrifices repeated endlessly year after year, make perfect those who draw near to worship.
texts.KJV: For the law having a shadow of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
texts.NASB95: For the Law, since it has only a shadow of the good things to come and not the very form of things, can never, by the same sacrifices which they offer continually year by year, make perfect those who draw near.
texts.NKJV: For the law, having a shadow of the good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with these same sacrifices, which they offer continually year by year, make those who approach perfect.
texts.DRB: For the law having a shadow of the good things to come, not the very image of the things; by the selfsame sacrifices which they offer continually every year, can never make the comers thereunto perfect:
texts.ESV: For since the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered every year, make perfect those who draw near.
texts.LEB: For the law, possessing a shadow of the good things that are about to come, not the form of things itself, is never able year by year by means of the same sacrifices which they offer without interruption to make perfect those who draw near.
texts.AMP: For since the Law has only a shadow [just a pale representation] of the good things to come—not the very image of those things—it can never, by offering the same sacrifices continually year after year, make perfect those who approach [its altars].
texts.NLT: The old system under the law of Moses was only a shadow, a dim preview of the good things to come, not the good things themselves. The sacrifices under that system were repeated again and again, year after year, but they were never able to provide perfect cleansing for those who came to worship.
texts.AKJV: For the law having a shadow of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
texts.NRSV: Since the law has only a shadow of the good things to come and not the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered year after year, make perfect those who approach.

texts.NIV84: If it could, would they not have stopped being offered? For the worshipers would have been cleansed once for all, and would no longer have felt guilty for their sins.
texts.KJV: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
texts.NASB95: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have had consciousness of sins?
texts.NKJV: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? For the worshipers, once purified, would have had no more consciousness of sins.
texts.DRB: For then they would have ceased to be offered: because the worshippers once cleansed should have no conscience of sin any longer:
texts.ESV: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, since the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
texts.LEB: For otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, because the ones who worship, having been purified once and for all, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
texts.AMP: For if it were otherwise, would not these sacrifices have stopped being offered? For the worshipers, having once [for all time] been cleansed, would no longer have a consciousness of sin.
texts.NLT: If they could have provided perfect cleansing, the sacrifices would have stopped, for the worshipers would have been purified once for all time, and their feelings of guilt would have disappeared.
texts.AKJV: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
texts.NRSV: Otherwise, would they not have ceased being offered, since the worshipers, cleansed once for all, would no longer have any consciousness of sin?

texts.NIV84: But those sacrifices are an annual reminder of sins,
texts.KJV: But in those sacrifices there is a remembrance again made of sins every year.
texts.NASB95: But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year by year.
texts.NKJV: But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
texts.DRB: But in them there is made a commemoration of sins every year.
texts.ESV: But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
texts.LEB: But in them there is a reminder of sins year by year.
texts.AMP: But [as it is] these [continual] sacrifices bring a fresh reminder of sins [to be atoned for] year after year,
texts.NLT: But instead, those sacrifices actually reminded them of their sins year after year.
texts.AKJV: But in those sacrifices there is a remembrance again made of sins every year.
texts.NRSV: But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sin year after year.

texts.NIV84: because it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.KJV: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
texts.NASB95: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.NKJV: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and goats could take away sins.
texts.DRB: For it is impossible that with the blood of oxen and goats sin should be taken away.
texts.ESV: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.LEB: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.AMP: for it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.NLT: For it is not possible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
texts.AKJV: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
texts.NRSV: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.

texts.NIV84: Therefore, when Christ came into the world, he said: "Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you prepared for me;
texts.KJV: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:
texts.NKJV:
Therefore, when He came into the world, He said:
“Sacrifice and offering You did not desire,
But a body You have prepared for Me.
texts.DRB: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith: Sacrifice and oblation thou wouldest not: but a body thou hast fitted to me:
texts.AMP: Therefore, when Christ enters into the world, He says, “SACRIFICE AND OFFERING YOU HAVE NOT DESIRED, BUT [instead] YOU HAVE PREPARED A BODY FOR ME [to offer];
texts.NLT:
That is why, when Christ came into the world, he said to God,
“You did not want animal sacrifices or sin offerings.
    But you have given me a body to offer.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:

texts.NIV84: with burnt offerings and sin offerings you were not pleased.
texts.KJV: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
texts.NASB95: In whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin You have taken no pleasure.
texts.NKJV:
In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin
You had no pleasure.
texts.DRB: Holocausts for sin did not please thee.
texts.ESV:
in burnt offerings and sin offerings
    you have taken no pleasure.
texts.LEB: you did not delight in whole burnt offerings and offerings for sins.

You were not pleased with burnt offerings
    or other offerings for sin.
texts.AKJV: in burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
texts.NRSV:
in burnt offerings and sin offerings
    you have taken no pleasure.

texts.NIV84: Then I said, 'Here I am--it is written about me in the scroll--I have come to do your will, O God.'"
texts.KJV: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
texts.NASB95:
“Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come
(In the scroll of the book it is written of Me)
To do Your will, O God.’”
texts.NKJV:
Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come—
In the volume of the book it is written of Me—
To do Your will, O God.’ ”
texts.DRB: Then said I: Behold I come: in the head of the book it is written of me: that I should do thy will, O God.
texts.ESV:
Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come to do your will, O God,
    as it is written of me in the scroll of the book.’”
texts.LEB:
Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come—
in the roll of the book it is written about me—
    to do your will, O God.’
texts.AMP: “THEN I SAID, ‘BEHOLD, I HAVE COME TO DO YOUR WILL, O GOD— [TO FULFILL] WHAT IS WRITTEN OF ME IN THE SCROLL OF THE BOOK.’”
texts.NLT:
Then I said, ‘Look, I have come to do your will, O God—
    as is written about me in the Scriptures.’”
texts.AKJV: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
texts.NRSV:
Then I said, ‘See, I have come to do your will, O God’
    (in the scroll of the book it is written of me).”

texts.NIV84: First he said, "Sacrifices and offerings, burnt offerings and sin offerings you did not desire, nor were you pleased with them" (although the law required them to be made).
texts.KJV: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
texts.NASB95: After saying above, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin You have not desired, nor have You taken pleasure in them” (which are offered according to the Law),
texts.NKJV: Previously saying, “Sacrifice and offering, burnt offerings, and offerings for sin You did not desire, nor had pleasure in them” (which are offered according to the law),
texts.DRB: In saying before, Sacrifices, and oblations, and holocausts for sin thou wouldest not, neither are they pleasing to thee, which are offered according to the law.
texts.ESV: When he said above, “You have neither desired nor taken pleasure in sacrifices and offerings and burnt offerings and sin offerings” (these are offered according to the law),
texts.LEB:
When he says above,
“Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and offerings for sin
    you did not want, nor did you delight in,”
which are offered according to the law,
texts.AMP: After saying [in the citation] above, “YOU HAVE NEITHER DESIRED, NOR HAVE YOU TAKEN DELIGHT IN SACRIFICES AND OFFERINGS AND WHOLE BURNT OFFERINGS AND sacrifices FOR SIN” (which are offered according to the Law)
texts.NLT: First, Christ said, “You did not want animal sacrifices or sin offerings or burnt offerings or other offerings for sin, nor were you pleased with them” (though they are required by the law of Moses).
texts.AKJV: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
texts.NRSV: When he said above, “You have neither desired nor taken pleasure in sacrifices and offerings and burnt offerings and sin offerings” (these are offered according to the law),

texts.NIV84: Then he said, "Here I am, I have come to do your will." He sets aside the first to establish the second.
texts.KJV: Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.
texts.NASB95: then He said, “Behold, I have come to do Your will.” He takes away the first in order to establish the second.
texts.NKJV: then He said, “Behold, I have come to do Your will, O God.” He takes away the first that He may establish the second.
texts.DRB: Then said I: Behold, I come to do thy will, O God: he taketh away the first, that he may establish that which followeth.
texts.ESV: then he added, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He does away with the first in order to establish the second.
texts.LEB:
then he has said,
“Behold, I have come to do your will.”
He takes away the first in order to establish the second,
texts.AMP: then He said, “BEHOLD, I HAVE COME TO DO YOUR WILL.” [And so] He does away with the first [covenant as a means of atoning for sin based on animal sacrifices] so that He may inaugurate and establish the second [covenant by means of obedience].
texts.NLT: Then he said, “Look, I have come to do your will.” He cancels the first covenant in order to put the second into effect.
texts.AKJV: then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.
texts.NRSV: then he added, “See, I have come to do your will.” He abolishes the first in order to establish the second.

texts.NIV84: And by that will, we have been made holy through the sacrifice of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.KJV: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.NASB95: By this will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.NKJV: By that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.DRB: In the which will, we are sanctified by the oblation of the body of Jesus Christ once.
texts.ESV: And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.LEB: by which will we are made holy through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.AMP: And in accordance with this will [of God] we [who believe in the message of salvation] have been sanctified [that is, set apart as holy for God and His purposes] through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) once for all.
texts.NLT: For God’s will was for us to be made holy by the sacrifice of the body of Jesus Christ, once for all time.
texts.AKJV: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
texts.NRSV: And it is by God’s will that we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

texts.NIV84: Day after day every priest stands and performs his religious duties; again and again he offers the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
texts.KJV: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:
texts.NASB95: Every priest stands daily ministering and offering time after time the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins;
texts.NKJV: And every priest stands ministering daily and offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
texts.DRB: And every priest indeed standeth daily ministering, and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
texts.ESV: And every priest stands daily at his service, offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
texts.LEB: And every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices many times, which are never able to take away sins.
texts.AMP: Every priest stands [at his altar of service] ministering daily, offering the same sacrifices over and over, which are never able to strip away sins [that envelop and cover us];
texts.NLT: Under the old covenant, the priest stands and ministers before the altar day after day, offering the same sacrifices again and again, which can never take away sins.
texts.AKJV: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:
texts.NRSV: And every priest stands day after day at his service, offering again and again the same sacrifices that can never take away sins.

texts.NIV84: But when this priest had offered for all time one sacrifice for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God.

texts.NASB95: but He, having offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, sat down at the right hand of God,
texts.NKJV: But this Man, after He had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God,
texts.DRB: But this man offering one sacrifice for sins, for ever sitteth on the right hand of God,
texts.ESV: But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God,
texts.LEB: But this one, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, sat down at the right hand of God,
texts.AMP: whereas Christ, having offered the one sacrifice [the all-sufficient sacrifice of Himself] for sins for all time, SAT DOWN [signifying the completion of atonement for sin] AT THE RIGHT HAND OF GOD [the position of honor],
texts.NLT: But our High Priest offered himself to God as a single sacrifice for sins, good for all time. Then he sat down in the place of honor at God’s right hand.
texts.AKJV: but this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;
texts.NRSV: But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins, “he sat down at the right hand of God,”

“This is the covenant that I will make with them
After those days, says the Lord:

texts.AKJV: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.

texts.NIV84: let us draw near to God with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled to cleanse us from a guilty conscience and having our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.KJV: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.NASB95: let us draw near with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.NKJV: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.DRB: Let us draw near with a true heart in fulness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with clean water.
texts.ESV: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.LEB: let us approach with a true heart in the full assurance of faith, our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
texts.AMP: let us approach [God] with a true and sincere heart in unqualified assurance of faith, having had our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.

texts.NRSV: let us approach with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.

texts.NIV84: Let us hold unswervingly to the hope we profess, for he who promised is faithful.
texts.KJV: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
texts.NASB95: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is faithful;
texts.NKJV: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is faithful.
texts.DRB: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering (for he is faithful that hath promised),
texts.ESV: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who promised is faithful.
texts.LEB: Let us hold fast to the confession of our hope without wavering, for the one who promised is faithful.
texts.AMP: Let us seize and hold tightly the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is reliable and trustworthy and faithful [to His word];
texts.NLT: Let us hold tightly without wavering to the hope we affirm, for God can be trusted to keep his promise.
texts.AKJV: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
texts.NRSV: Let us hold fast to the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who has promised is faithful.

texts.NRSV: And let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good deeds,

texts.NIV84: Let us not give up meeting together, as some are in the habit of doing, but let us encourage one another--and all the more as you see the Day approaching.

texts.NKJV: not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as is the manner of some, but exhorting one another, and so much the more as you see the Day approaching.

texts.NRSV: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day approaching.

texts.LEB: For if we keep on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,

texts.DRB: A man making void the law of Moses, dieth without any mercy under two or three witnesses:
texts.ESV: Anyone who has set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
texts.LEB: Anyone who rejected the law of Moses dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
texts.AMP: Anyone who has ignored and set aside the Law of Moses is put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
texts.NLT: For anyone who refused to obey the law of Moses was put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
texts.AKJV: He that despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:
texts.NRSV: Anyone who has violated the law of Moses dies without mercy “on the testimony of two or three witnesses.”

texts.NIV84: How much more severely do you think a man deserves to be punished who has trampled the Son of God under foot, who has treated as an unholy thing the blood of the covenant that sanctified him, and who has insulted the Spirit of grace?
texts.KJV: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?
texts.NASB95: How much severer punishment do you think he will deserve who has trampled under foot the Son of God, and has regarded as unclean the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?
texts.NKJV: Of how much worse punishment, do you suppose, will he be thought worthy who has trampled the Son of God underfoot, counted the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified a common thing, and insulted the Spirit of grace?
texts.DRB: How much more, do you think he deserveth worse punishments, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath esteemed the blood of the testament unclean, by which he was sanctified, and hath offered an affront to the Spirit of grace?
texts.ESV: How much worse punishment, do you think, will be deserved by the one who has trampled underfoot the Son of God, and has profaned the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and has outraged the Spirit of grace?
texts.LEB: How much worse punishment do you think the person will be considered worthy of who treats with disdain the Son of God and who considers ordinary the blood of the covenant by which he was made holy and who insults the Spirit of grace?
texts.AMP: How much greater punishment do you think he will deserve who has rejected and trampled under foot the Son of God, and has considered unclean and common the blood of the covenant that sanctified him, and has insulted the Spirit of grace [who imparts the unmerited favor and blessing of God]?
texts.NLT: Just think how much worse the punishment will be for those who have trampled on the Son of God, and have treated the blood of the covenant, which made us holy, as if it were common and unholy, and have insulted and disdained the Holy Spirit who brings God’s mercy to us.
texts.AKJV: of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?
texts.NRSV: How much worse punishment do you think will be deserved by those who have spurned the Son of God, profaned the blood of the covenant by which they were sanctified, and outraged the Spirit of grace?

texts.NIV84: For we know him who said, "It is mine to avenge; I will repay," and again, "The Lord will judge his people."
texts.KJV: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
texts.NASB95: For we know Him who said, “Vengeance is Mine, I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge His people.”
texts.NKJV: For we know Him who said, “Vengeance is Mine, I will repay,” says the Lord. And again, “The Lord will judge His people.”
texts.DRB: For we know him that hath said: Vengeance belongeth to me, and I will repay. And again: The Lord shall judge his people.
texts.ESV: For we know him who said, “Vengeance is mine; I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
texts.LEB:
For we know the one who said,
“Vengeance is mine, I will repay,”
and again,
“The Lord will judge his people.”
texts.AMP: For we know Him who said, “VENGEANCE IS MINE [retribution and the deliverance of justice rest with Me], I WILL REPAY [the wrongdoer].” And again, “THE LORD WILL JUDGE HIS PEOPLE.”
texts.NLT:
For we know the one who said,
“I will take revenge.
    I will pay them back.”
He also said,
“The Lord will judge his own people.”
texts.AKJV: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
texts.NRSV: For we know the one who said, “Vengeance is mine; I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge his people.”

texts.NIV84: It is a dreadful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.KJV: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.NASB95: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.NKJV: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.DRB: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.ESV: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.LEB: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.AMP: It is a fearful and terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God [incurring His judgment and wrath].
texts.NLT: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.AKJV: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
texts.NRSV: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
