texts.NIV84: Remember those earlier days after you had received the light, when you stood your ground in a great contest in the face of suffering.
texts.KJV: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;
texts.NASB95: But remember the former days, when, after being enlightened, you endured a great conflict of sufferings,
texts.NKJV: But recall the former days in which, after you were illuminated, you endured a great struggle with sufferings:
texts.DRB: But call to mind the former days, wherein, being illuminated, you endured a great fight of afflictions.
texts.ESV: But recall the former days when, after you were enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings,
texts.LEB: But remember the former days in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings,
texts.AMP: But remember the earlier days, when, after being [spiritually] enlightened, you [patiently] endured a great conflict of sufferings,
texts.NLT: Think back on those early days when you first learned about Christ. Remember how you remained faithful even though it meant terrible suffering.
texts.AKJV: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;
texts.NRSV: But recall those earlier days when, after you had been enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings,

texts.NIV84: Sometimes you were publicly exposed to insult and persecution; at other times you stood side by side with those who were so treated.
texts.KJV: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
texts.NASB95: partly by being made a public spectacle through reproaches and tribulations, and partly by becoming sharers with those who were so treated.
texts.NKJV: partly while you were made a spectacle both by reproaches and tribulations, and partly while you became companions of those who were so treated;
texts.DRB: And on the one hand indeed, by reproaches and tribulations, were made a gazingstock; and on the other, became companions of them that were used in such sort.
texts.ESV: sometimes being publicly exposed to reproach and affliction, and sometimes being partners with those so treated.
texts.LEB: sometimes being publicly exposed both to insults and to afflictions, and sometimes becoming sharers with those who were treated in this way.
texts.AMP: sometimes by being made a spectacle, publicly exposed to insults and distress, and sometimes by becoming companions with those who were so treated.
texts.NLT: Sometimes you were exposed to public ridicule and were beaten, and sometimes you helped others who were suffering the same things.
texts.AKJV: partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
texts.NRSV: sometimes being publicly exposed to insults and afflictions and sometimes becoming partners with those so treated.

texts.NIV84: You sympathized with those in prison and joyfully accepted the confiscation of your property, because you knew that you yourselves had better and lasting possessions.
texts.KJV: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.
texts.NASB95: For you showed sympathy to the prisoners and accepted joyfully the seizure of your property, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and a lasting one.
texts.NKJV: for you had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your goods, knowing that you have a better and an enduring possession for yourselves in heaven.
texts.DRB: For you both had compassion on them that were in bands, and took with joy the being stripped of your own goods, knowing that you have a better and a lasting substance.
texts.ESV: For you had compassion on those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the plundering of your property, since you knew that you yourselves had a better possession and an abiding one.
texts.LEB: For you both sympathized with the prisoners and put up with the seizure of your belongings with joy because you knew that you yourselves had a better and permanent possession.
texts.AMP: For you showed sympathy and deep concern for those who were imprisoned, and you joyfully accepted the [unjust] seizure of your belongings and the confiscation of your property, conscious of the fact that you have a better possession and a lasting one [prepared for you in heaven].
texts.NLT: You suffered along with those who were thrown into jail, and when all you owned was taken from you, you accepted it with joy. You knew there were better things waiting for you that will last forever.
texts.AKJV: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.
texts.NRSV: For you had compassion for those who were in prison, and you cheerfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you yourselves possessed something better and more lasting.

texts.NIV84: So do not throw away your confidence; it will be richly rewarded.
texts.KJV: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
texts.NKJV: Therefore do not cast away your confidence, which has great reward.
texts.DRB: Do not therefore lose your confidence, which hath a great reward.
texts.ESV: Therefore do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
texts.LEB: Therefore do not throw away your confidence, which has great reward.
texts.AMP: Do not, therefore, fling away your [fearless] confidence, for it has a glorious and great reward.
texts.NLT: So do not throw away this confident trust in the Lord. Remember the great reward it brings you!
texts.AKJV: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
texts.NRSV: Do not, therefore, abandon that boldness of yours; it brings a great reward.

texts.NIV84: You need to persevere so that when you have done the will of God, you will receive what he has promised.
texts.KJV: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
texts.NASB95: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God, you may receive what was promised.
texts.NKJV: For you have need of endurance, so that after you have done the will of God, you may receive the promise:
texts.DRB: For patience is necessary for you; that, doing the will of God, you may receive the promise.
texts.ESV: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God you may receive what is promised.
texts.LEB: For you have need of endurance, in order that after you have done the will of God, you may receive what was promised.
texts.AMP: For you have need of patient endurance [to bear up under difficult circumstances without compromising], so that when you have carried out the will of God, you may receive and enjoy to the full what is promised.
texts.NLT: Patient endurance is what you need now, so that you will continue to do God’s will. Then you will receive all that he has promised.
texts.AKJV: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
texts.NRSV: For you need endurance, so that when you have done the will of God you may receive what was promised.

texts.NIV84: For in just a very little while, "He who is coming will come and will not delay.
texts.KJV: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
texts.NASB95:
For yet in a very little while,
He who is coming will come, and will not delay.
texts.NKJV:
“For yet a little while,
And He who is coming will come and will not tarry.
texts.DRB: For yet a little and a very little while, and he that is to come, will come, and will not delay.
texts.LEB:
For yet
“a very, very little while,
    and the one who is coming will come and will not delay.
texts.AMP: FOR YET IN A VERY LITTLE WHILE, HE WHO IS COMING WILL COME, AND WILL NOT DELAY.
texts.NLT:
“For in just a little while,
    the Coming One will come and not delay.
texts.AKJV: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
texts.NRSV:
For yet
“in a very little while,
    the one who is coming will come and will not delay,

texts.NIV84: But my righteous one will live by faith. And if he shrinks back, I will not be pleased with him."
texts.KJV: Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
texts.NASB95:
But My righteous one shall live by faith;
And if he shrinks back, My soul has no pleasure in him.
texts.DRB: But my just man liveth by faith; but if he withdraw himself, he shall not please my soul.
texts.ESV:
but my righteous one shall live by faith,
    and if he shrinks back,
my soul has no pleasure in him.”
texts.LEB:
But my righteous one will live by faith,
    and if he shrinks back, my soul is not well pleased with him.”
texts.AMP: BUT MY RIGHTEOUS ONE [the one justified by faith] SHALL LIVE BY FAITH [respecting man’s relationship to God and trusting Him]; AND IF HE DRAWS BACK [shrinking in fear], MY SOUL HAS NO DELIGHT IN HIM.
texts.NLT:
And my righteous ones will live by faith.
    But I will take no pleasure in anyone who turns away.”
texts.AKJV: Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
texts.NRSV:
but my righteous one will live by faith.
    My soul takes no pleasure in anyone who shrinks back.”

texts.NIV84: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who believe and are saved.
texts.KJV: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.
texts.NASB95: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the preserving of the soul.
texts.NKJV: But we are not of those who draw back to perdition, but of those who believe to the saving of the soul.
texts.DRB: But we are not the children of withdrawing unto perdition, but of faith to the saving of the soul.
texts.ESV: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who have faith and preserve their souls.
texts.LEB: But we are not among those who shrink back to destruction, but among those who have faith to the preservation of our souls.
texts.AMP: But our way is not that of those who shrink back to destruction, but [we are] of those who believe [relying on God through faith in Jesus Christ, the Messiah] and by this confident faith preserve the soul.
texts.NLT: But we are not like those who turn away from God to their own destruction. We are the faithful ones, whose souls will be saved.
texts.AKJV: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.
texts.NRSV: But we are not among those who shrink back and so are lost but among those who have faith and so preserve our souls.

texts.NIV84: Wives, in the same way be submissive to your husbands so that, if any of them do not believe the word, they may be won over without words by the behavior of their wives,
texts.KJV: Likewise, ye wives, be in subjection to your own husbands; that, if any obey not the word, they also may without the word be won by the conversation of the wives;
texts.NASB95: In the same way, you wives, be submissive to your own husbands so that even if any of them are disobedient to the word, they may be won without a word by the behavior of their wives,
texts.NKJV: Wives, likewise, be submissive to your own husbands, that even if some do not obey the word, they, without a word, may be won by the conduct of their wives,
texts.DRB: In like manner also let wives be subject to their husbands: that if any believe not the word, they may be won without the word, by the conversation of the wives.
texts.ESV: Likewise, wives, be subject to your own husbands, so that even if some do not obey the word, they may be won without a word by the conduct of their wives,
texts.LEB: In the same way, wives, be subject to your own husbands, so that even if some are disobedient to the word, they may be won over without a word by the conduct of their wives,
texts.AMP: In the same way, you wives, be submissive to your own husbands [subordinate, not as inferior, but out of respect for the responsibilities entrusted to husbands and their accountability to God, and so partnering with them] so that even if some do not obey the word [of God], they may be won over [to Christ] without discussion by the godly lives of their wives,
texts.NLT: In the same way, you wives must accept the authority of your husbands. Then, even if some refuse to obey the Good News, your godly lives will speak to them without any words. They will be won over
texts.AKJV: Likewise, ye wives, be in subjection to your own husbands; that, if any obey not the word, they also may without the word be won by the conversation of the wives;
texts.NRSV: Wives, in the same way, be subject to your husbands, so that, even if some of them do not obey the word, they may be won over without a word by their wives’ conduct,

texts.NIV84: when they see the purity and reverence of your lives.
texts.KJV: While they behold your chaste conversation coupled with fear.
texts.NASB95: as they observe your chaste and respectful behavior.
texts.NKJV: when they observe your chaste conduct accompanied by fear.
texts.DRB: Considering your chaste conversation with fear.
texts.ESV: when they see your respectful and pure conduct.
texts.LEB: when they see your respectful, pure conduct.
texts.AMP: when they see your modest and respectful behavior [together with your devotion and appreciation—love your husband, encourage him, and enjoy him as a blessing from God].
texts.NLT: by observing your pure and reverent lives.
texts.AKJV: while they behold your chaste conversation coupled with fear.
texts.NRSV: when they see the purity and respect of your conduct.

texts.NIV84: Your beauty should not come from outward adornment, such as braided hair and the wearing of gold jewelry and fine clothes.
texts.KJV: Whose adorning let it not be that outward adorning of plaiting the hair, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparel;
texts.NASB95: Your adornment must not be merely external—braiding the hair, and wearing gold jewelry, or putting on dresses;
texts.NKJV: Do not let your adornment be merely outward—arranging the hair, wearing gold, or putting on fine apparel—
texts.DRB: Whose adorning let it not be the outward plaiting of the hair, or the wearing of gold, or the putting on of apparel:
texts.ESV: Do not let your adorning be external—the braiding of hair and the putting on of gold jewelry, or the clothing you wear—
texts.LEB: Let your adornment not be the external kind, braiding hair and putting on gold jewelry or putting on fine clothing,
texts.AMP: Your adornment must not be merely external—with interweaving and elaborate knotting of the hair, and wearing gold jewelry, or [being superficially preoccupied with] dressing in expensive clothes;
texts.NLT: Don’t be concerned about the outward beauty of fancy hairstyles, expensive jewelry, or beautiful clothes.
texts.AKJV: Whose adorning let it not be that outward adorning of plaiting the hair, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparel;
texts.NRSV: Do not adorn yourselves outwardly by braiding your hair and by wearing gold ornaments or fine clothing;

texts.NIV84: Instead, it should be that of your inner self, the unfading beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is of great worth in God's sight.
texts.KJV: But let it be the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.
texts.NASB95: but let it be the hidden person of the heart, with the imperishable quality of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is precious in the sight of God.
texts.NKJV: rather let it be the hidden person of the heart, with the incorruptible beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in the sight of God.
texts.DRB: But the hidden man of the heart in the incorruptibility of a quiet and a meek spirit, which is rich in the sight of God.
texts.ESV: but let your adorning be the hidden person of the heart with the imperishable beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which in God's sight is very precious.
texts.LEB: but the hidden person of the heart, with the imperishable quality of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is highly valuable in the sight of God.
texts.AMP: but let it be [the inner beauty of] the hidden person of the heart, with the imperishable quality and unfading charm of a gentle and peaceful spirit, [one that is calm and self-controlled, not overanxious, but serene and spiritually mature] which is very precious in the sight of God.
texts.NLT: You should clothe yourselves instead with the beauty that comes from within, the unfading beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is so precious to God.
texts.AKJV: but let it be the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.
texts.NRSV: rather, let your adornment be the inner self with the lasting beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in God’s sight.

texts.NIV84: For this is the way the holy women of the past who put their hope in God used to make themselves beautiful. They were submissive to their own husbands,
texts.KJV: For after this manner in the old time the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection unto their own husbands:
texts.NASB95: For in this way in former times the holy women also, who hoped in God, used to adorn themselves, being submissive to their own husbands;
texts.NKJV: For in this manner, in former times, the holy women who trusted in God also adorned themselves, being submissive to their own husbands,
texts.DRB: For after this manner heretofore the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection to their own husbands:
texts.ESV: For this is how the holy women who hoped in God used to adorn themselves, by submitting to their own husbands,
texts.LEB: For in the same way formerly the holy women also, who hoped in God, used to adorn themselves by being subject to their own husbands,
texts.AMP: For in this way in former times the holy women, who hoped in God, used to adorn themselves, being submissive to their own husbands and adapting themselves to them;
texts.NLT: This is how the holy women of old made themselves beautiful. They put their trust in God and accepted the authority of their husbands.
texts.AKJV: For after this manner in the old time the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection unto their own husbands:
texts.NRSV: It was in this way long ago that the holy women who hoped in God used to adorn themselves by being subject to their husbands.

texts.NIV84: like Sarah, who obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her daughters if you do what is right and do not give way to fear.
texts.KJV: Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any amazement.
texts.NASB95: just as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, and you have become her children if you do what is right without being frightened by any fear.
texts.NKJV: as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose daughters you are if you do good and are not afraid with any terror.
texts.DRB: As Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters you are, doing well, and not fearing any disturbance.
texts.ESV: as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord. And you are her children, if you do good and do not fear anything that is frightening.
texts.LEB: like Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose children you have become when you do good and are not frightened with respect to any terror.
texts.AMP: just as Sarah obeyed Abraham [following him and having regard for him as head of their house], calling him lord. And you have become her daughters if you do what is right without being frightened by any fear [that is, being respectful toward your husband but not giving in to intimidation, nor allowing yourself to be led into sin, nor to be harmed].
texts.NLT: For instance, Sarah obeyed her husband, Abraham, and called him her master. You are her daughters when you do what is right without fear of what your husbands might do.
texts.AKJV: even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any amazement.
texts.NRSV: Thus Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him lord. You have become her daughters as long as you do what is good and never let fears alarm you.

texts.NIV84: Husbands, in the same way be considerate as you live with your wives, and treat them with respect as the weaker partner and as heirs with you of the gracious gift of life, so that nothing will hinder your prayers.
texts.KJV: Likewise, ye husbands, dwell with them according to knowledge, giving honour unto the wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life; that your prayers be not hindered.
texts.NASB95: You husbands in the same way, live with your wives in an understanding way, as with someone weaker, since she is a woman; and show her honor as a fellow heir of the grace of life, so that your prayers will not be hindered.
texts.NKJV: Husbands, likewise, dwell with them with understanding, giving honor to the wife, as to the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life, that your prayers may not be hindered.
texts.DRB: Ye husbands, likewise dwelling with them according to knowledge, giving honour to the female as to the weaker vessel, and as to the co-heirs of the grace of life: that your prayers be not hindered.
texts.ESV: Likewise, husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way, showing honor to the woman as the weaker vessel, since they are heirs with you of the grace of life, so that your prayers may not be hindered.
texts.LEB: Husbands, in the same way live with your wives knowledgeably, as with the weaker female vessel, showing them honor as fellow heirs also of the grace of life, so that your prayers will not be hindered.
texts.AMP: In the same way, you husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way [with great gentleness and tact, and with an intelligent regard for the marriage relationship], as with someone physically weaker, since she is a woman. Show her honor and respect as a fellow heir of the grace of life, so that your prayers will not be hindered or ineffective.
texts.NLT: In the same way, you husbands must give honor to your wives. Treat your wife with understanding as you live together. She may be weaker than you are, but she is your equal partner in God’s gift of new life. Treat her as you should so your prayers will not be hindered.
texts.AKJV: Likewise, ye husbands, dwell with them according to knowledge, giving honour unto the wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life; that your prayers be not hindered.
texts.NRSV: Husbands, in the same way, show consideration for your wives in your life together, paying honor to the woman—though the weaker vessel, they are joint heirs of the gracious gift of life—so that nothing may hinder your prayers.

texts.NIV84: Finally, all of you, live in harmony with one another; be sympathetic, love as brothers, be compassionate and humble.
texts.KJV: Finally, be ye all of one mind, having compassion one of another, love as brethren, be pitiful, be courteous:
texts.NASB95: To sum up, all of you be harmonious, sympathetic, brotherly, kindhearted, and humble in spirit;
texts.NKJV: Finally, all of you be of one mind, having compassion for one another; love as brothers, be tenderhearted, be courteous;
texts.DRB: And in fine, be ye all of one mind, having compassion one of another, being lovers of the brotherhood, merciful, modest, humble:
texts.ESV: Finally, all of you, have unity of mind, sympathy, brotherly love, a tender heart, and a humble mind.
texts.LEB: And finally, all of you be harmonious, sympathetic, showing mutual affection, compassionate, humble,
texts.AMP: Finally, all of you be like-minded [united in spirit], sympathetic, brotherly, kindhearted [courteous and compassionate toward each other as members of one household], and humble in spirit;
texts.NLT: Finally, all of you should be of one mind. Sympathize with each other. Love each other as brothers and sisters. Be tenderhearted, and keep a humble attitude.
texts.AKJV: Finally, be ye all of one mind, having compassion one of another, love as brethren, be pitiful, be courteous:
texts.NRSV: Finally, all of you, have unity of spirit, sympathy, love for one another, a tender heart, and a humble mind.

texts.NIV84: Do not repay evil with evil or insult with insult, but with blessing, because to this you were called so that you may inherit a blessing.
texts.KJV: Not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing.
texts.NASB95: not returning evil for evil or insult for insult, but giving a blessing instead; for you were called for the very purpose that you might inherit a blessing.
texts.NKJV: not returning evil for evil or reviling for reviling, but on the contrary blessing, knowing that you were called to this, that you may inherit a blessing.
texts.DRB: Not rendering evil for evil, nor railing for railing, but contrariwise, blessing: for unto this are you called, that you may inherit a blessing.
texts.ESV: Do not repay evil for evil or reviling for reviling, but on the contrary, bless, for to this you were called, that you may obtain a blessing.
texts.LEB: not repaying evil for evil or insult for insult, but on the other hand blessing others, because for this reason you were called, so that you could inherit a blessing.
texts.AMP: and never return evil for evil or insult for insult [avoid scolding, berating, and any kind of abuse], but on the contrary, give a blessing [pray for one another’s well-being, contentment, and protection]; for you have been called for this very purpose, that you might inherit a blessing [from God that brings well-being, happiness, and protection].
texts.NLT: Don’t repay evil for evil. Don’t retaliate with insults when people insult you. Instead, pay them back with a blessing. That is what God has called you to do, and he will grant you his blessing.
texts.AKJV: not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing.
texts.NRSV: Do not repay evil for evil or abuse for abuse, but, on the contrary, repay with a blessing. It is for this that you were called—that you might inherit a blessing.

texts.NIV84: For, "Whoever would love life and see good days must keep his tongue from evil and his lips from deceitful speech.
texts.KJV: For he that will love life, and see good days, let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips that they speak no guile:
texts.NASB95:
For,
“The one who desires life, to love and see good days,
Must keep his tongue from evil and his lips from speaking deceit.
texts.NKJV:
For
“He who would love life
And see good days,
Let him refrain his tongue from evil,
And his lips from speaking deceit.
texts.DRB: For he that will love life, and see good days, let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips that they speak no guile.
texts.ESV:
For
“Whoever desires to love life
    and see good days,
let him keep his tongue from evil
    and his lips from speaking deceit;
texts.LEB:
For
“The one who wants to love life
    and see good days
must keep his tongue from evil
    and his lips must not speak deceit.
texts.AMP: For, “THE ONE WHO WANTS TO ENJOY LIFE AND SEE GOOD DAYS [good—whether apparent or not], MUST KEEP HIS TONGUE FREE FROM EVIL AND HIS LIPS FROM SPEAKING GUILE (treachery, deceit).
texts.NLT:
For the Scriptures say,
“If you want to enjoy life
    and see many happy days,
keep your tongue from speaking evil
    and your lips from telling lies.
texts.AKJV: For he that will love life, and see good days, let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips that they speak no guile:
texts.NRSV:
For
“Those who desire to love life
    and to see good days,
let them keep their tongues from evil
    and their lips from speaking deceit;

texts.NIV84: He must turn from evil and do good; he must seek peace and pursue it.
texts.KJV: Let him eschew evil, and do good; let him seek peace, and ensue it.
texts.NASB95:
“He must turn away from evil and do good;
He must seek peace and pursue it.
texts.NKJV:
Let him turn away from evil and do good;
Let him seek peace and pursue it.
texts.DRB: Let him decline from evil, and do good: let him seek after peace and pursue it:
texts.ESV:
let him turn away from evil and do good;
    let him seek peace and pursue it.
texts.LEB:
And he must turn away from evil and do good;
    he must seek peace and pursue it.
texts.AMP: “HE MUST TURN AWAY FROM WICKEDNESS AND DO WHAT IS RIGHT. HE MUST SEARCH FOR PEACE [with God, with self, with others] AND PURSUE IT EAGERLY [actively—not merely desiring it].
texts.NLT:
Turn away from evil and do good.
    Search for peace, and work to maintain it.
texts.AKJV: let him eschew evil, and do good; let him seek peace, and ensue it.
texts.NRSV:
let them turn away from evil and do good;
    let them seek peace and pursue it.

texts.NIV84: For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous and his ears are attentive to their prayer, but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil."
texts.KJV: For the eyes of the Lord are over the righteous, and his ears are open unto their prayers: but the face of the Lord is against them that do evil.
texts.NASB95:
“For the eyes of the Lord are toward the righteous,
And His ears attend to their prayer,
But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”
texts.DRB: Because the eyes of the Lord are upon the just, and his ears unto their prayers: but the countenance of the Lord upon them that do evil things.
texts.ESV:
For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous,
    and his ears are open to their prayer.
But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”
texts.LEB:
For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous,
    and his ears are open to their prayer.
But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.
texts.AMP: “FOR THE EYES OF THE LORD ARE [looking favorably] UPON THE RIGHTEOUS (the upright), AND HIS EARS ARE ATTENTIVE TO THEIR PRAYER (eager to answer), BUT THE FACE OF THE LORD IS AGAINST THOSE WHO PRACTICE EVIL.”
texts.NLT:
The eyes of the Lord watch over those who do right,
    and his ears are open to their prayers.
But the Lord turns his face
    against those who do evil.”
texts.AKJV: For the eyes of the Lord are over the righteous, and his ears are open unto their prayers: but the face of the Lord is against them that do evil.
texts.NRSV:
For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous,
    and his ears are open to their prayer.
But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”

texts.NIV84: Who is going to harm you if you are eager to do good?
texts.KJV: And who is he that will harm you, if ye be followers of that which is good?
texts.NASB95: Who is there to harm you if you prove zealous for what is good?
texts.NKJV: And who is he who will harm you if you become followers of what is good?
texts.DRB: And who is he that can hurt you, if you be zealous of good?
texts.ESV: Now who is there to harm you if you are zealous for what is good?
texts.LEB: And who is the one who will harm you if you are a zealous adherent for what is good?
texts.AMP: Now who is there to hurt you if you become enthusiastic for what is good?
texts.NLT: Now, who will want to harm you if you are eager to do good?
texts.AKJV: And who is he that will harm you, if ye be followers of that which is good?
texts.NRSV: Now who will harm you if you are eager to do what is good?

texts.NIV84: But even if you should suffer for what is right, you are blessed. "Do not fear what they fear; do not be frightened."
texts.KJV: But and if ye suffer for righteousness' sake, happy are ye: and be not afraid of their terror, neither be troubled;
texts.NASB95: But even if you should suffer for the sake of righteousness, you are blessed. And do not fear their intimidation, and do not be troubled,
texts.NKJV: But even if you should suffer for righteousness’ sake, you are blessed. “And do not be afraid of their threats, nor be troubled.”
texts.DRB: But if also you suffer any thing for justice' sake, blessed are ye. And be not afraid of their fear, and be not troubled.
texts.ESV: But even if you should suffer for righteousness' sake, you will be blessed. Have no fear of them, nor be troubled,
texts.LEB: But even if you might suffer for the sake of righteousness, you are blessed. And do not be afraid of their intimidation or be disturbed,
texts.AMP: But even if you should suffer for the sake of righteousness [though it is not certain that you will], you are still blessed [happy, to be admired and favored by God]. DO NOT BE AFRAID OF THEIR INTIMIDATING THREATS, NOR BE TROUBLED or DISTURBED [by their opposition].
texts.NLT: But even if you suffer for doing what is right, God will reward you for it. So don’t worry or be afraid of their threats.
texts.AKJV: But and if ye suffer for righteousness’ sake, happy are ye: and be not afraid of their terror, neither be troubled;
texts.NRSV: But even if you do suffer for doing what is right, you are blessed. Do not fear what they fear, and do not be intimidated,

texts.NIV84: But in your hearts set apart Christ as Lord. Always be prepared to give an answer to everyone who asks you to give the reason for the hope that you have. But do this with gentleness and respect,
texts.KJV: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:
texts.NASB95: but sanctify Christ as Lord in your hearts, always being ready to make a defense to everyone who asks you to give an account for the hope that is in you, yet with gentleness and reverence;
texts.NKJV: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts, and always be ready to give a defense to everyone who asks you a reason for the hope that is in you, with meekness and fear;
texts.DRB: But sanctify the Lord Christ in your hearts, being ready always to satisfy every one that asketh you a reason of that hope which is in you.
texts.ESV: but in your hearts honor Christ the Lord as holy, always being prepared to make a defense to anyone who asks you for a reason for the hope that is in you; yet do it with gentleness and respect,
texts.LEB: but set Christ apart as Lord in your hearts, always ready to make a defense to anyone who asks you for an accounting concerning the hope that is in you.
texts.AMP: But in your hearts set Christ apart [as holy—acknowledging Him, giving Him first place in your lives] as Lord. Always be ready to give a [logical] defense to anyone who asks you to account for the hope and confident assurance [elicited by faith] that is within you, yet [do it] with gentleness and respect.
texts.NLT: Instead, you must worship Christ as Lord of your life. And if someone asks about your hope as a believer, always be ready to explain it.
texts.AKJV: but sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:
texts.NRSV: but in your hearts sanctify Christ as Lord. Always be ready to make your defense to anyone who demands from you an accounting for the hope that is in you,

texts.NIV84: keeping a clear conscience, so that those who speak maliciously against your good behavior in Christ may be ashamed of their slander.
texts.KJV: Having a good conscience; that, whereas they speak evil of you, as of evildoers, they may be ashamed that falsely accuse your good conversation in Christ.
texts.NASB95: and keep a good conscience so that in the thing in which you are slandered, those who revile your good behavior in Christ will be put to shame.
texts.NKJV: having a good conscience, that when they defame you as evildoers, those who revile your good conduct in Christ may be ashamed.
texts.DRB: But with modesty and fear, having a good conscience: that whereas they speak evil of you, they may be ashamed who falsely accuse your good conversation in Christ.
texts.ESV: having a good conscience, so that, when you are slandered, those who revile your good behavior in Christ may be put to shame.
texts.LEB: But do so with courtesy and respect, having a good conscience, so that in the things in which you are slandered, the ones who malign your good conduct in Christ may be put to shame.
texts.AMP: And see to it that your conscience is entirely clear, so that every time you are slandered or falsely accused, those who attack or disparage your good behavior in Christ will be shamed [by their own words].
texts.NLT: But do this in a gentle and respectful way. Keep your conscience clear. Then if people speak against you, they will be ashamed when they see what a good life you live because you belong to Christ.
texts.AKJV: having a good conscience; that, whereas they speak evil of you, as of evildoers, they may be ashamed that falsely accuse your good conversation in Christ.
texts.NRSV: yet do it with gentleness and respect. Maintain a good conscience so that, when you are maligned, those who abuse you for your good conduct in Christ may be put to shame.

texts.NIV84: It is better, if it is God's will, to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
texts.KJV: For it is better, if the will of God be so, that ye suffer for well doing, than for evil doing.
texts.NASB95: For it is better, if God should will it so, that you suffer for doing what is right rather than for doing what is wrong.
texts.NKJV: For it is better, if it is the will of God, to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
texts.DRB: For it is better doing well (if such be the will of God) to suffer, than doing ill.
texts.ESV: For it is better to suffer for doing good, if that should be God's will, than for doing evil.
texts.LEB: For it is better to suffer for doing good, if God wills it, than for doing evil.
texts.AMP: For it is better that you suffer [unjustly] for doing what is right, if that should be God’s will, than [to suffer justly] for doing wrong.
texts.NLT: Remember, it is better to suffer for doing good, if that is what God wants, than to suffer for doing wrong!
texts.AKJV: For it is better, if the will of God be so, that ye suffer for well doing, than for evil doing.
texts.NRSV: For it is better to suffer for doing good, if suffering should be God’s will, than to suffer for doing evil.

texts.NIV84: For Christ died for sins once for all, the righteous for the unrighteous, to bring you to God. He was put to death in the body but made alive by the Spirit,
texts.KJV: For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit:
texts.NASB95: For Christ also died for sins once for all, the just for the unjust, so that He might bring us to God, having been put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit;
texts.NKJV: For Christ also suffered once for sins, the just for the unjust, that He might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but made alive by the Spirit,
texts.DRB: Because Christ also died once for our sins, the just for the unjust: that he might offer us to God, being put to death indeed in the flesh, but enlivened in the spirit,
texts.ESV: For Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but made alive in the spirit,
texts.LEB:
For Christ also suffered once for sins,
    the just for the unjust,
in order that he could bring you to God,
    being put to death in the flesh,
but made alive in the spirit,
texts.AMP: For indeed Christ died for sins once for all, the Just and Righteous for the unjust and unrighteous [the Innocent for the guilty] so that He might bring us to God, having been put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the Spirit;
texts.NLT: Christ suffered for our sins once for all time. He never sinned, but he died for sinners to bring you safely home to God. He suffered physical death, but he was raised to life in the Spirit.
texts.AKJV: For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit:
texts.NRSV: For Christ also suffered for sins once for all, the righteous for the unrighteous, in order to bring you to God. He was put to death in the flesh but made alive in the spirit,

texts.NIV84: through whom also he went and preached to the spirits in prison
texts.KJV: By which also he went and preached unto the spirits in prison;
texts.NASB95: in which also He went and made proclamation to the spirits now in prison,
texts.NKJV: by whom also He went and preached to the spirits in prison,
texts.DRB: In which also coming he preached to those spirits that were in prison:
texts.ESV: in which he went and proclaimed to the spirits in prison,
texts.LEB: in which also he went and proclaimed to the spirits in prison,
texts.AMP: in which He also went and preached to the spirits now in prison,
texts.NLT: So he went and preached to the spirits in prison—
texts.AKJV: by which also he went and preached unto the spirits in prison;
texts.NRSV: in which also he went and made a proclamation to the spirits in prison,

texts.NIV84: who disobeyed long ago when God waited patiently in the days of Noah while the ark was being built. In it only a few people, eight in all, were saved through water,
texts.KJV: Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were saved by water.
texts.NASB95: who once were disobedient, when the patience of God kept waiting in the days of Noah, during the construction of the ark, in which a few, that is, eight persons, were brought safely through the water.
texts.NKJV: who formerly were disobedient, when once the Divine longsuffering waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared, in which a few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.
texts.DRB: Which had been some time incredulous, when they waited for the patience of God in the days of Noe, when the ark was a building: wherein a few, that is, eight souls, were saved by water.
texts.ESV: because they formerly did not obey, when God's patience waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared, in which a few, that is, eight persons, were brought safely through water.
texts.LEB: who were formerly disobedient, when the patience of God waited in the days of Noah, while an ark was being constructed, in which a few—that is, eight souls—were rescued through water.
texts.AMP: who once were disobedient, when the great patience of God was waiting in the days of Noah, during the building of the ark, in which a few, that is, eight persons [Noah’s family], were brought safely through the water.
texts.NLT: those who disobeyed God long ago when God waited patiently while Noah was building his boat. Only eight people were saved from drowning in that terrible flood.
texts.AKJV: which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were saved by water.
texts.NRSV: who in former times did not obey, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah, during the building of the ark, in which a few, that is, eight lives, were saved through water.

texts.NIV84: and this water symbolizes baptism that now saves you also--not the removal of dirt from the body but the pledge of a good conscience toward God. It saves you by the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.KJV: The like figure whereunto even baptism doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ:
texts.NASB95: Corresponding to that, baptism now saves you—not the removal of dirt from the flesh, but an appeal to God for a good conscience—through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.NKJV: There is also an antitype which now saves us—baptism (not the removal of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God), through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.DRB: Whereunto baptism being of the like form, now saveth you also: not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the examination of a good conscience towards God by the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
texts.ESV: Baptism, which corresponds to this, now saves you, not as a removal of dirt from the body but as an appeal to God for a good conscience, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.LEB: And also, corresponding to this, baptism now saves you, not the removal of dirt from the flesh, but an appeal to God for a good conscience through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.AMP: Corresponding to that [rescue through the flood], baptism [which is an expression of a believer’s new life in Christ] now saves you, not by removing dirt from the body, but by an appeal to God for a good (clear) conscience, [demonstrating what you believe to be yours] through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
texts.NLT: And that water is a picture of baptism, which now saves you, not by removing dirt from your body, but as a response to God from a clean conscience. It is effective because of the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
texts.AKJV: The like figure whereunto even baptism doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ:
texts.NRSV: And baptism, which this prefigured, now saves you—not as a removal of dirt from the body but as an appeal to God for a good conscience, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,

texts.NIV84: who has gone into heaven and is at God's right hand--with angels, authorities and powers in submission to him.
texts.KJV: Who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him.
texts.NASB95: who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, after angels and authorities and powers had been subjected to Him.
texts.NKJV: who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, angels and authorities and powers having been made subject to Him.
texts.DRB: Who is on the right hand of God, swallowing down death, that we might be made heirs of life everlasting: being gone into heaven, the angels and powers and virtues being made subject to him.
texts.ESV: who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, with angels, authorities, and powers having been subjected to him.
texts.LEB: who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, with angels and authorities and powers having been subjected to him.
texts.AMP: who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God [that is, the place of honor and authority], with [all] angels and authorities and powers made subservient to Him.
texts.NLT: Now Christ has gone to heaven. He is seated in the place of honor next to God, and all the angels and authorities and powers accept his authority.
texts.AKJV: who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him.
texts.NRSV: who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, with angels, authorities, and powers made subject to him.

texts.NIV84: I want you to know how much I am struggling for you and for those at Laodicea, and for all who have not met me personally.
texts.KJV: For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you, and for them at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
texts.NASB95: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have on your behalf and for those who are at Laodicea, and for all those who have not personally seen my face,
texts.NKJV: For I want you to know what a great conflict I have for you and those in Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh,
texts.DRB: For I would have you know, what manner of care I have for you and for them that are at Laodicea, and whosoever have not seen my face in the flesh:
texts.ESV: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those at Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face,
texts.LEB: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have on behalf of you, and those in Laodicea, and all those who have not seen my face in person,
texts.AMP: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those [believers] at Laodicea, and for all who [like yourselves] have never seen me face to face.
texts.NLT: I want you to know how much I have agonized for you and for the church at Laodicea, and for many other believers who have never met me personally.
texts.AKJV: For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you, and for them at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
texts.NRSV: For I want you to know how greatly I strive for you and for those in Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face.

texts.NIV84: My purpose is that they may be encouraged in heart and united in love, so that they may have the full riches of complete understanding, in order that they may know the mystery of God, namely, Christ,
texts.KJV: That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;
texts.NASB95: that their hearts may be encouraged, having been knit together in love, and attaining to all the wealth that comes from the full assurance of understanding, resulting in a true knowledge of God’s mystery, that is, Christ Himself,
texts.NKJV: that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, and attaining to all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the knowledge of the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ,
texts.DRB: That their hearts may be comforted, being instructed in charity, and unto all riches of fulness of understanding, unto the knowledge of the mystery of God the Father and of Christ Jesus:
texts.ESV: that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, to reach all the riches of full assurance of understanding and the knowledge of God's mystery, which is Christ,
texts.LEB: so that their hearts may be encouraged, united in love and into all the wealth of the full assurance of insight into the knowledge of the mystery of God, Christ,
texts.AMP: [For my hope is] that their hearts may be encouraged as they are knit together in [unselfish] love, so that they may have all the riches that come from the full assurance of understanding [the joy of salvation], resulting in a true [and more intimate] knowledge of the mystery of God, that is, Christ,
texts.NLT: I want them to be encouraged and knit together by strong ties of love. I want them to have complete confidence that they understand God’s mysterious plan, which is Christ himself.
texts.AKJV: that their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;
texts.NRSV: I want their hearts to be encouraged and united in love, so that they may have all the riches of assured understanding and have the knowledge of God’s mystery, that is, Christ,

texts.NIV84: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.KJV: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.NASB95: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.NKJV: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.DRB: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.ESV: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.LEB: in whom all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden.
texts.AMP: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge [regarding the word and purposes of God].
texts.NLT: In him lie hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.AKJV: in whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
texts.NRSV: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.

texts.NIV84: I tell you this so that no one may deceive you by fine-sounding arguments.
texts.KJV: And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
texts.NASB95: I say this so that no one will delude you with persuasive argument.
texts.NKJV: Now this I say lest anyone should deceive you with persuasive words.
texts.DRB: Now this I say, that no man may deceive you by loftiness of words.
texts.ESV: I say this in order that no one may delude you with plausible arguments.
texts.LEB: I say this in order that no one will deceive you with persuasive speech,
texts.AMP: I say this so that no one will deceive you with persuasive [but thoroughly deceptive] arguments.
texts.NLT: I am telling you this so no one will deceive you with well-crafted arguments.
texts.AKJV: And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
texts.NRSV: I am saying this so that no one may deceive you with plausible arguments.

texts.NIV84: For though I am absent from you in body, I am present with you in spirit and delight to see how orderly you are and how firm your faith in Christ is.
texts.KJV: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the stedfastness of your faith in Christ.
texts.NASB95: For even though I am absent in body, nevertheless I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good discipline and the stability of your faith in Christ.
texts.NKJV: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.
texts.DRB: For though I be absent in body, yet in spirit I am with you; rejoicing, and beholding your order, and the steadfastness of your faith which is in Christ.
texts.ESV: For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
texts.LEB: for even if I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing and seeing your good order and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.
texts.AMP: For even though I am absent [from you] in body, nevertheless I am with you in spirit, delighted to see your good discipline [as you stand shoulder to shoulder and form a solid front] and to see the stability of your faith in Christ [your steadfast reliance on Him and your unwavering confidence in His power, wisdom, and goodness].
texts.NLT: For though I am far away from you, my heart is with you. And I rejoice that you are living as you should and that your faith in Christ is strong.
texts.AKJV: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the stedfastness of your faith in Christ.
texts.NRSV: For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, and I rejoice to see your orderly conduct and the firmness of your faith in Christ.

texts.NIV84: So then, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live in him,
texts.KJV: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:
texts.NASB95: Therefore as you have received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in Him,
texts.NKJV: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in Him,
texts.DRB: As therefore you have received Jesus Christ the Lord, walk ye in him;
texts.ESV: Therefore, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in him,
texts.LEB: Therefore as you have received Christ Jesus the Lord, live in him,
texts.AMP: Therefore as you have received Christ Jesus the Lord, walk in [union with] Him [reflecting His character in the things you do and say—living lives that lead others away from sin],
texts.NLT: And now, just as you accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord, you must continue to follow him.
texts.AKJV: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:
texts.NRSV: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, continue to walk in him,

texts.NIV84: rooted and built up in him, strengthened in the faith as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
texts.KJV: Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.
texts.NASB95: having been firmly rooted and now being built up in Him and established in your faith, just as you were instructed, and overflowing with gratitude.
texts.NKJV: rooted and built up in Him and established in the faith, as you have been taught, abounding in it with thanksgiving.
texts.DRB: Rooted and built up in him, and confirmed in the faith, as also you have learned, abounding in him in thanksgiving.
texts.ESV: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
texts.LEB: firmly rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding with thankfulness.
texts.AMP: having been deeply rooted [in Him] and now being continually built up in Him and [becoming increasingly more] established in your faith, just as you were taught, and overflowing in it with gratitude.
texts.NLT: Let your roots grow down into him, and let your lives be built on him. Then your faith will grow strong in the truth you were taught, and you will overflow with thankfulness.
texts.AKJV: rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.
texts.NRSV: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.

texts.NIV84: See to it that no one takes you captive through hollow and deceptive philosophy, which depends on human tradition and the basic principles of this world rather than on Christ.
texts.KJV: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.
texts.NASB95: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deception, according to the tradition of men, according to the elementary principles of the world, rather than according to Christ.
texts.NKJV: Beware lest anyone cheat you through philosophy and empty deceit, according to the tradition of men, according to the basic principles of the world, and not according to Christ.
texts.DRB: Beware lest any man cheat you by philosophy, and vain deceit; according to the tradition of men, according to the elements of the world, and not according to Christ:
texts.ESV: See to it that no one takes you captive by philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits of the world, and not according to Christ.
texts.LEB: Beware lest anyone take you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits of the world and not according to Christ,
texts.AMP: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deception [pseudo-intellectual babble], according to the tradition [and musings] of mere men, following the elementary principles of this world, rather than following [the truth—the teachings of] Christ.
texts.NLT: Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ.
texts.AKJV: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.
texts.NRSV: Watch out that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental principles of the world, and not according to Christ.

texts.NIV84: For in Christ all the fullness of the Deity lives in bodily form,
texts.KJV: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
texts.NASB95: For in Him all the fullness of Deity dwells in bodily form,
texts.NKJV: For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Godhead bodily;
texts.DRB: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead corporeally;
texts.ESV: For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily,
texts.LEB: because in him all the fullness of deity dwells bodily,
texts.AMP: For in Him all the fullness of Deity (the Godhead) dwells in bodily form [completely expressing the divine essence of God].
texts.NLT: For in Christ lives all the fullness of God in a human body.
texts.AKJV: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
texts.NRSV: For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily,

texts.NIV84: and you have been given fullness in Christ, who is the head over every power and authority.
texts.KJV: And ye are complete in him, which is the head of all principality and power:
texts.NASB95: and in Him you have been made complete, and He is the head over all rule and authority;
texts.NKJV: and you are complete in Him, who is the head of all principality and power.
texts.DRB: And you are filled in him, who is the head of all principality and power:
texts.ESV: and you have been filled in him, who is the head of all rule and authority.
texts.LEB: and you are filled in him, who is the head over every ruler and authority,
texts.AMP: And in Him you have been made complete [achieving spiritual stature through Christ], and He is the head over all rule and authority [of every angelic and earthly power].
texts.NLT: So you also are complete through your union with Christ, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
texts.AKJV: And ye are complete in him, which is the head of all principality and power:
texts.NRSV: and you have come to fullness in him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.

texts.NIV84: In him you were also circumcised, in the putting off of the sinful nature, not with a circumcision done by the hands of men but with the circumcision done by Christ,
texts.KJV: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:
texts.NASB95: and in Him you were also circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, in the removal of the body of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ;
texts.NKJV: In Him you were also circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, by putting off the body of the sins of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ,
texts.DRB: In whom also you are circumcised with circumcision not made by hand, in despoiling of the body of the flesh, but in the circumcision of Christ:
texts.ESV: In him also you were circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, by putting off the body of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ,
texts.LEB: in whom also you were circumcised with a circumcision not made by hands, by the removal of the body of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ,
texts.AMP: In Him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, but by the [spiritual] circumcision of Christ in the stripping off of the body of the flesh [the sinful carnal nature],
texts.NLT: When you came to Christ, you were “circumcised,” but not by a physical procedure. Christ performed a spiritual circumcision—the cutting away of your sinful nature.
texts.AKJV: in whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:
texts.NRSV: In him also you were circumcised with a spiritual circumcision, by the removal of the body of the flesh in the circumcision of Christ;

texts.NIV84: having been buried with him in baptism and raised with him through your faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
texts.KJV: Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.
texts.NASB95: having been buried with Him in baptism, in which you were also raised up with Him through faith in the working of God, who raised Him from the dead.
texts.NKJV: buried with Him in baptism, in which you also were raised with Him through faith in the working of God, who raised Him from the dead.
texts.DRB: Buried with him in baptism, in whom also you are risen again by the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him up from the dead.
texts.ESV: having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the powerful working of God, who raised him from the dead.
texts.LEB: having been buried with him in baptism, in which also you were raised together with him through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.
texts.AMP: having been buried with Him in baptism and raised with Him [to a new life] through [your] faith in the working of God, [as displayed] when He raised Christ from the dead.
texts.NLT: For you were buried with Christ when you were baptized. And with him you were raised to new life because you trusted the mighty power of God, who raised Christ from the dead.
texts.AKJV: buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.
texts.NRSV: when you were buried with him in baptism, you were also raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.

texts.NIV84: When you were dead in your sins and in the uncircumcision of your sinful nature, God made you alive with Christ. He forgave us all our sins,
texts.KJV: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;
texts.NASB95: When you were dead in your transgressions and the uncircumcision of your flesh, He made you alive together with Him, having forgiven us all our transgressions,
texts.NKJV: And you, being dead in your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, He has made alive together with Him, having forgiven you all trespasses,
texts.DRB: And you, when you were dead in your sins, and the uncircumcision of your flesh; he hath quickened together with him, forgiving you all offences:
texts.ESV: And you, who were dead in your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, God made alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,
texts.LEB: And although you were dead in the trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, he made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,
texts.AMP: When you were dead in your sins and in the uncircumcision of your flesh (worldliness, manner of life), God made you alive together with Christ, having [freely] forgiven us all our sins,
texts.NLT: You were dead because of your sins and because your sinful nature was not yet cut away. Then God made you alive with Christ, for he forgave all our sins.
texts.AKJV: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;
texts.NRSV: And when you were dead in trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, God made you alive together with him, when he forgave us all our trespasses,

texts.NIV84: having canceled the written code, with its regulations, that was against us and that stood opposed to us; he took it away, nailing it to the cross.
texts.KJV: Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;
texts.NASB95: having canceled out the certificate of debt consisting of decrees against us, which was hostile to us; and He has taken it out of the way, having nailed it to the cross.
texts.NKJV: having wiped out the handwriting of requirements that was against us, which was contrary to us. And He has taken it out of the way, having nailed it to the cross.
texts.DRB: Blotting out the handwriting of the decree that was against us, which was contrary to us. And he hath taken the same out of the way, fastening it to the cross:
texts.ESV: by canceling the record of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. This he set aside, nailing it to the cross.
texts.LEB: having destroyed the certificate of indebtedness in ordinances against us, which was hostile to us, and removed it out of the way by nailing it to the cross.
texts.AMP: having canceled out the certificate of debt consisting of legal demands [which were in force] against us and which were hostile to us. And this certificate He has set aside and completely removed by nailing it to the cross.
texts.NLT: He canceled the record of the charges against us and took it away by nailing it to the cross.
texts.AKJV: blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;
texts.NRSV: erasing the record that stood against us with its legal demands. He set this aside, nailing it to the cross.

texts.NIV84: And having disarmed the powers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross.
texts.KJV: And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
texts.NASB95: When He had disarmed the rulers and authorities, He made a public display of them, having triumphed over them through Him.
texts.NKJV: Having disarmed principalities and powers, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it.
texts.DRB: And despoiling the principalities and powers, he hath exposed them confidently in open shew, triumphing over them in himself.
texts.ESV: He disarmed the rulers and authorities and put them to open shame, by triumphing over them in him.
texts.LEB: When he had disarmed the rulers and the authorities, he made a display of them in public, triumphing over them by it.
texts.AMP: When He had disarmed the rulers and authorities [those supernatural forces of evil operating against us], He made a public example of them [exhibiting them as captives in His triumphal procession], having triumphed over them through the cross.
texts.NLT: In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross.
texts.AKJV: and having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
texts.NRSV: He disarmed the rulers and authorities and made a public example of them, triumphing over them in it.

texts.NIV84: Therefore do not let anyone judge you by what you eat or drink, or with regard to a religious festival, a New Moon celebration or a Sabbath day.
texts.KJV: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:
texts.NASB95: Therefore no one is to act as your judge in regard to food or drink or in respect to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath day—
texts.NKJV: So let no one judge you in food or in drink, or regarding a festival or a new moon or sabbaths,
texts.DRB: Let no man therefore judge you in meat or in drink, or in respect of a festival day, or of the new moon, or of the sabbaths,
texts.ESV: Therefore let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food and drink, or with regard to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath.
texts.LEB: Therefore do not let anyone judge you with reference to eating or drinking or participation in a feast or a new moon or a Sabbath,
texts.AMP: Therefore let no one judge you in regard to food and drink or in regard to [the observance of] a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath day.
texts.NLT: So don’t let anyone condemn you for what you eat or drink, or for not celebrating certain holy days or new moon ceremonies or Sabbaths.
texts.AKJV: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:
texts.NRSV: Therefore, do not let anyone condemn you in matters of food or drink or of observing festivals, new moons, or Sabbaths.

texts.NIV84: These are a shadow of the things that were to come; the reality, however, is found in Christ.
texts.KJV: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.
texts.NASB95: things which are a mere shadow of what is to come; but the substance belongs to Christ.
texts.NKJV: which are a shadow of things to come, but the substance is of Christ.
texts.DRB: Which are a shadow of things to come, but the body is of Christ.
texts.ESV: These are a shadow of the things to come, but the substance belongs to Christ.
texts.LEB: which are a shadow of what is to come, but the reality is Christ.
texts.AMP: Such things are only a shadow of what is to come and they have only symbolic value; but the substance [the reality of what is foreshadowed] belongs to Christ.
texts.NLT: For these rules are only shadows of the reality yet to come. And Christ himself is that reality.
texts.AKJV: which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.
texts.NRSV: These are only a shadow of what is to come, but the body belongs to Christ.

texts.NIV84: Do not let anyone who delights in false humility and the worship of angels disqualify you for the prize. Such a person goes into great detail about what he has seen, and his unspiritual mind puffs him up with idle notions.
texts.KJV: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
texts.NASB95: Let no one keep defrauding you of your prize by delighting in self-abasement and the worship of the angels, taking his stand on visions he has seen, inflated without cause by his fleshly mind,
texts.NKJV: Let no one cheat you of your reward, taking delight in false humility and worship of angels, intruding into those things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
texts.DRB: Let no man seduce you, willing in humility, and religion of angels, walking in the things which he hath not seen, in vain puffed up by the sense of his flesh,
texts.ESV: Let no one disqualify you, insisting on asceticism and worship of angels, going on in detail about visions, puffed up without reason by his sensuous mind,
texts.LEB: Let no one condemn you, taking pleasure in humility and the worship of angels, going into detail about the things which he has seen, inflated without cause by his fleshly mind,
texts.AMP: Let no one defraud you of your prize [your freedom in Christ and your salvation] by insisting on mock humility and the worship of angels, going into detail about visions [he claims] he has seen [to justify his authority], puffed up [in conceit] by his unspiritual mind,
texts.NLT: Don’t let anyone condemn you by insisting on pious self-denial or the worship of angels, saying they have had visions about these things. Their sinful minds have made them proud,
texts.AKJV: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
texts.NRSV: Do not let anyone disqualify you, insisting on self-abasement and worship of angels, initiatory visions, puffed up without cause by a human way of thinking,

texts.NIV84: He has lost connection with the Head, from whom the whole body, supported and held together by its ligaments and sinews, grows as God causes it to grow.
texts.KJV: And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.
texts.NASB95: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the entire body, being supplied and held together by the joints and ligaments, grows with a growth which is from God.
texts.NKJV: and not holding fast to the Head, from whom all the body, nourished and knit together by joints and ligaments, grows with the increase that is from God.
texts.DRB: And not holding the head, from which the whole body, by joints and bands, being supplied with nourishment and compacted, groweth unto the increase of God.
texts.ESV: and not holding fast to the Head, from whom the whole body, nourished and knit together through its joints and ligaments, grows with a growth that is from God.
texts.LEB: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the whole body, supported and held together by the ligaments and sinews, grows with the growth of God.
texts.AMP: and not holding fast to the head [of the body, Jesus Christ], from whom the entire body, supplied and knit together by its joints and ligaments, grows with the growth [that can come only] from God.
texts.NLT: and they are not connected to Christ, the head of the body. For he holds the whole body together with its joints and ligaments, and it grows as God nourishes it.
texts.AKJV: and not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.
texts.NRSV: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the whole body, nourished and held together by its ligaments and tendons, grows with a growth that is from God.

texts.NIV84: Since you died with Christ to the basic principles of this world, why, as though you still belonged to it, do you submit to its rules:
texts.KJV: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,
texts.NASB95: If you have died with Christ to the elementary principles of the world, why, as if you were living in the world, do you submit yourself to decrees, such as,
texts.NKJV: Therefore, if you died with Christ from the basic principles of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to regulations—
texts.DRB: If then you be dead with Christ from the elements of this world, why do you yet decree as though living in the world?
texts.ESV: If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the world, why, as if you were still alive in the world, do you submit to regulations—
texts.LEB: If you have died with Christ to the elemental spirits of the world, why do you submit to them as if living in the world?
texts.AMP: If you have died with Christ to the elementary principles of the world, why, as if you were still living in the world, do you submit to rules and regulations, such as,
texts.NLT: You have died with Christ, and he has set you free from the spiritual powers of this world. So why do you keep on following the rules of the world, such as,
texts.AKJV: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,
texts.NRSV: If with Christ you died to the elemental principles of the world, why do you live as if you still belonged to the world? Why do you submit to regulations,

texts.NIV84: "Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!"?
texts.KJV: (Touch not; taste not; handle not;
