texts.LEB: until we all reach the unity of the faith and the knowledge of the Son of God, to a mature man, to a measure of the maturity of the fullness of Christ,
texts.AMP: until we all reach oneness in the faith and in the knowledge of the Son of God, [growing spiritually] to become a mature believer, reaching to the measure of the fullness of Christ [manifesting His spiritual completeness and exercising our spiritual gifts in unity].
texts.NLT: This will continue until we all come to such unity in our faith and knowledge of God’s Son that we will be mature in the Lord, measuring up to the full and complete standard of Christ.
texts.AKJV: till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:
texts.NRSV: until all of us come to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to maturity, to the measure of the full stature of Christ.

texts.NIV84: Then we will no longer be infants, tossed back and forth by the waves, and blown here and there by every wind of teaching and by the cunning and craftiness of men in their deceitful scheming.
texts.KJV: That we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;
texts.NASB95: As a result, we are no longer to be children, tossed here and there by waves and carried about by every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, by craftiness in deceitful scheming;
texts.NKJV: that we should no longer be children, tossed to and fro and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in the cunning craftiness of deceitful plotting,
texts.DRB: That henceforth we be no more children tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine by the wickedness of men, by cunning craftiness, by which they lie in wait to deceive
texts.ESV: so that we may no longer be children, tossed to and fro by the waves and carried about by every wind of doctrine, by human cunning, by craftiness in deceitful schemes.
texts.LEB: so that we may no longer be infants, tossed about by waves and carried about by every wind of teaching, by the trickery of people, by craftiness with reference to the scheming of deceit.
texts.AMP: So that we are no longer children [spiritually immature], tossed back and forth [like ships on a stormy sea] and carried about by every wind of [shifting] doctrine, by the cunning and trickery of [unscrupulous] men, by the deceitful scheming of people ready to do anything [for personal profit].
texts.NLT: Then we will no longer be immature like children. We won’t be tossed and blown about by every wind of new teaching. We will not be influenced when people try to trick us with lies so clever they sound like the truth.
texts.AKJV: that we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;
texts.NRSV: We must no longer be children, tossed to and fro and blown about by every wind of doctrine by people’s trickery, by their craftiness in deceitful scheming;

texts.NIV84: Instead, speaking the truth in love, we will in all things grow up into him who is the Head, that is, Christ.
texts.KJV: But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, even Christ:
texts.NASB95: but speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in all aspects into Him who is the head, even Christ,
texts.NKJV: but, speaking the truth in love, may grow up in all things into Him who is the head—Christ—
texts.DRB: But doing the truth in charity, we may in all things grow up in him who is the head, even Christ:
texts.ESV: Rather, speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in every way into him who is the head, into Christ,
texts.LEB: But speaking the truth in love, we are to grow into him with reference to all things, who is the head, Christ,
texts.AMP: But speaking the truth in love [in all things—both our speech and our lives expressing His truth], let us grow up in all things into Him [following His example] who is the Head—Christ.
texts.NLT: Instead, we will speak the truth in love, growing in every way more and more like Christ, who is the head of his body, the church.
texts.AKJV: but speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, even Christ:
texts.NRSV: but speaking the truth in love, we must grow up in every way into him who is the head, into Christ,

texts.NIV84: From him the whole body, joined and held together by every supporting ligament, grows and builds itself up in love, as each part does its work.
texts.KJV: From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.
texts.NASB95: from whom the whole body, being fitted and held together by what every joint supplies, according to the proper working of each individual part, causes the growth of the body for the building up of itself in love.
texts.NKJV: from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by what every joint supplies, according to the effective working by which every part does its share, causes growth of the body for the edifying of itself in love.
texts.DRB: From whom the whole body, being compacted and fitly joined together, by what every joint supplieth, according to the operation in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body, unto the edifying of itself in charity.
texts.ESV: from whom the whole body, joined and held together by every joint with which it is equipped, when each part is working properly, makes the body grow so that it builds itself up in love.
texts.LEB: from whom the whole body, joined together and held together by every supporting ligament, according to the working by measure of each single part, the growth of the body makes for the building up of itself in love.
texts.AMP: From Him the whole body [the church, in all its various parts], joined and knitted firmly together by what every joint supplies, when each part is working properly, causes the body to grow and mature, building itself up in [unselfish] love.
texts.NLT: He makes the whole body fit together perfectly. As each part does its own special work, it helps the other parts grow, so that the whole body is healthy and growing and full of love.
texts.AKJV: from whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.
texts.NRSV: from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by every ligament with which it is equipped, as each part is working properly, promotes the body’s growth in building itself up in love.

texts.NIV84: So I tell you this, and insist on it in the Lord, that you must no longer live as the Gentiles do, in the futility of their thinking.
texts.KJV: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,
texts.NASB95: So this I say, and affirm together with the Lord, that you walk no longer just as the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,
texts.NKJV: This I say, therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you should no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles walk, in the futility of their mind,
texts.DRB: This then I say and testify in the Lord: That henceforward you walk not as also the Gentiles walk in the vanity of their mind,
texts.ESV: Now this I say and testify in the Lord, that you must no longer walk as the Gentiles do, in the futility of their minds.
texts.LEB: This therefore I say and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the Gentiles walk: in the futility of their mind,
texts.AMP: So this I say, and solemnly affirm together with the Lord [as in His presence], that you must no longer live as the [unbelieving] Gentiles live, in the futility of their minds [and in the foolishness and emptiness of their souls],
texts.NLT: With the Lord’s authority I say this: Live no longer as the Gentiles do, for they are hopelessly confused.
texts.AKJV: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,
texts.NRSV: Now this I affirm and insist on in the Lord: you must no longer walk as the gentiles walk, in the futility of their minds;

texts.NIV84: They are darkened in their understanding and separated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them due to the hardening of their hearts.
texts.KJV: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:
texts.NASB95: being darkened in their understanding, excluded from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardness of their heart;
texts.NKJV: having their understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart;
texts.DRB: Having their understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their hearts.
texts.ESV: They are darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, due to their hardness of heart.
texts.LEB: being darkened in understanding, alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardness of their heart,
texts.AMP: for their [moral] understanding is darkened and their reasoning is clouded; [they are] alienated and self-banished from the life of God [with no share in it; this is] because of the [willful] ignorance and spiritual blindness that is [deep-seated] within them, because of the hardness and insensitivity of their heart.
texts.NLT: Their minds are full of darkness; they wander far from the life God gives because they have closed their minds and hardened their hearts against him.
texts.AKJV: having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:
texts.NRSV: they are darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God because of their ignorance and hardness of heart.

texts.NIV84: Having lost all sensitivity, they have given themselves over to sensuality so as to indulge in every kind of impurity, with a continual lust for more.
texts.KJV: Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
texts.NASB95: and they, having become callous, have given themselves over to sensuality for the practice of every kind of impurity with greediness.
texts.NKJV: who, being past feeling, have given themselves over to lewdness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
texts.DRB: Who despairing, have given themselves up to lasciviousness, unto the working of all uncleanness, unto the working of all uncleanness, unto covetousness.
texts.ESV: They have become callous and have given themselves up to sensuality, greedy to practice every kind of impurity.
texts.LEB: who, becoming callous, gave themselves over to licentiousness, for the pursuit of all uncleanness in greediness.
texts.AMP: And they, [the ungodly in their spiritual apathy], having become callous and unfeeling, have given themselves over [as prey] to unbridled sensuality, eagerly craving the practice of every kind of impurity [that their desires may demand].
texts.NLT: They have no sense of shame. They live for lustful pleasure and eagerly practice every kind of impurity.
texts.AKJV: who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
texts.NRSV: They have lost all sensitivity and have abandoned themselves to licentiousness, greedy to practice every kind of impurity.

texts.NIV84: You, however, did not come to know Christ that way.
texts.KJV: But ye have not so learned Christ;
texts.NASB95: But you did not learn Christ in this way,
texts.NKJV: But you have not so learned Christ,
texts.DRB: But you have not so learned Christ;
texts.ESV: But that is not the way you learned Christ!—
texts.LEB: But you did not learn Christ in this way,
texts.AMP: But you did not learn Christ in this way!
texts.NLT: But that isn’t what you learned about Christ.
texts.AKJV: But ye have not so learned Christ;
texts.NRSV: That is not the way you learned Christ!

texts.NIV84: Surely you heard of him and were taught in him in accordance with the truth that is in Jesus.
texts.KJV: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:
texts.NASB95: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught in Him, just as truth is in Jesus,
texts.NKJV: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught by Him, as the truth is in Jesus:
texts.DRB: If so be that you have heard him, and have been taught in him, as the truth is in Jesus:
texts.ESV: assuming that you have heard about him and were taught in him, as the truth is in Jesus,
texts.LEB: if indeed you have heard about him, and you were taught by him (just as truth is in Jesus),
texts.AMP: If in fact you have [really] heard Him and have been taught by Him, just as truth is in Jesus [revealed in His life and personified in Him],
texts.NLT: Since you have heard about Jesus and have learned the truth that comes from him,
texts.AKJV: if so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:
texts.NRSV: For surely you have heard about him and were taught in him, as truth is in Jesus,

texts.NIV84: You were taught, with regard to your former way of life, to put off your old self, which is being corrupted by its deceitful desires;
texts.KJV: That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;
texts.NASB95: that, in reference to your former manner of life, you lay aside the old self, which is being corrupted in accordance with the lusts of deceit,
texts.NKJV: that you put off, concerning your former conduct, the old man which grows corrupt according to the deceitful lusts,
texts.DRB: To put off, according to former conversation, the old man, who is corrupted according to the desire of error.
texts.ESV: to put off your old self, which belongs to your former manner of life and is corrupt through deceitful desires,
texts.LEB: that you take off, according to your former way of life, the old man, who is being destroyed according to deceitful desires,
texts.AMP: that, regarding your previous way of life, you put off your old self [completely discard your former nature], which is being corrupted through deceitful desires,
texts.NLT: throw off your old sinful nature and your former way of life, which is corrupted by lust and deception.
texts.AKJV: that ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;
texts.NRSV: to put away your former way of life, your old self, corrupt and deluded by its lusts,

texts.NIV84: to be made new in the attitude of your minds;
texts.KJV: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
texts.NASB95: and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
texts.NKJV: and be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
texts.DRB: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind:
texts.ESV: and to be renewed in the spirit of your minds,
texts.LEB: be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
texts.AMP: and be continually renewed in the spirit of your mind [having a fresh, untarnished mental and spiritual attitude],
texts.NLT: Instead, let the Spirit renew your thoughts and attitudes.
texts.AKJV: and be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
texts.NRSV: and to be renewed in the spirit of your minds,

texts.NIV84: and to put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
texts.KJV: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.
texts.NASB95: and put on the new self, which in the likeness of God has been created in righteousness and holiness of the truth.
texts.NKJV: and that you put on the new man which was created according to God, in true righteousness and holiness.
texts.DRB: And put on the new man, who according to God is created in justice and holiness of truth.
texts.ESV: and to put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness.
texts.LEB: and put on the new man (in accordance with God), who is created in righteousness and holiness from the truth.
texts.AMP: and put on the new self [the regenerated and renewed nature], created in God’s image, [godlike] in the righteousness and holiness of the truth [living in a way that expresses to God your gratitude for your salvation].
texts.NLT: Put on your new nature, created to be like God—truly righteous and holy.
texts.AKJV: and that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.
texts.NRSV: and to clothe yourselves with the new self, created according to the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness.

texts.NIV84: Therefore each of you must put off falsehood and speak truthfully to his neighbor, for we are all members of one body.
texts.KJV: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, laying aside falsehood, speak truth each one of you with his neighbor, for we are members of one another.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, putting away lying, “Let each one of you speak truth with his neighbor,” for we are members of one another.
texts.DRB: Wherefore putting away lying, speak ;ye the truth every man with his neighbour; for we are members one of another.
texts.ESV: Therefore, having put away falsehood, let each one of you speak the truth with his neighbor, for we are members one of another.
texts.LEB: Therefore, putting aside the lie, speak truth each one of you with his neighbor, because we are members of one another.
texts.AMP: Therefore, rejecting all falsehood [whether lying, defrauding, telling half-truths, spreading rumors, any such as these], SPEAK TRUTH EACH ONE WITH HIS NEIGHBOR, for we are all parts of one another [and we are all parts of the body of Christ].
texts.NLT: So stop telling lies. Let us tell our neighbors the truth, for we are all parts of the same body.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
texts.NRSV: So then, putting away falsehood, let each of you speak the truth with your neighbor, for we are members of one another.

texts.NIV84: "In your anger do not sin": Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry,
texts.KJV: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:
texts.NASB95: Be angry, and yet do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
texts.NKJV: “Be angry, and do not sin”: do not let the sun go down on your wrath,
texts.DRB: Be angry, and sin not. Let not the sun go down upon your anger.
texts.ESV: Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
texts.LEB: Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun set on your anger,
texts.AMP: BE ANGRY [at sin—at immorality, at injustice, at ungodly behavior], YET DO NOT SIN; do not let your anger [cause you shame, nor allow it to] last until the sun goes down.
texts.NLT: And “don’t sin by letting anger control you.” Don’t let the sun go down while you are still angry,
texts.AKJV: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:
texts.NRSV: Be angry but do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,

texts.NIV84: and do not give the devil a foothold.
texts.KJV: Neither give place to the devil.
texts.NASB95: and do not give the devil an opportunity.
texts.NKJV: nor give place to the devil.
texts.DRB: Give not place to the devil.
texts.ESV: and give no opportunity to the devil.
texts.LEB: nor give place to the devil.
texts.AMP: And do not give the devil an opportunity [to lead you into sin by holding a grudge, or nurturing anger, or harboring resentment, or cultivating bitterness].
texts.NLT: for anger gives a foothold to the devil.
texts.AKJV: neither give place to the devil.
texts.NRSV: and do not make room for the devil.

texts.NIV84: He who has been stealing must steal no longer, but must work, doing something useful with his own hands, that he may have something to share with those in need.
texts.KJV: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
texts.NASB95: He who steals must steal no longer; but rather he must labor, performing with his own hands what is good, so that he will have something to share with one who has need.
texts.NKJV: Let him who stole steal no longer, but rather let him labor, working with his hands what is good, that he may have something to give him who has need.
texts.DRB: He that stole, let him now steal no more; but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have something to give to him that suffereth need.
texts.ESV: Let the thief no longer steal, but rather let him labor, doing honest work with his own hands, so that he may have something to share with anyone in need.
texts.LEB: The one who steals must steal no longer, but instead must labor, working with his own hands what is good, so that he may have something to share with the one who has need.
texts.AMP: The thief [who has become a believer] must no longer steal, but instead he must work hard [making an honest living], producing that which is good with his own hands, so that he will have something to share with those in need.
texts.NLT: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Instead, use your hands for good hard work, and then give generously to others in need.
texts.AKJV: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
texts.NRSV: Those who steal must give up stealing; rather, let them labor, doing good work with their own hands, so as to have something to share with the needy.

texts.NIV84: Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
texts.KJV: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
texts.NASB95: Let no unwholesome word proceed from your mouth, but only such a word as is good for edification according to the need of the moment, so that it will give grace to those who hear.
texts.NKJV: Let no corrupt word proceed out of your mouth, but what is good for necessary edification, that it may impart grace to the hearers.
texts.DRB: Let no evil speech proceed from your mouth; but that which is good, to the edification of faith, that it may administer grace to the hearers.
texts.ESV: Let no corrupting talk come out of your mouths, but only such as is good for building up, as fits the occasion, that it may give grace to those who hear.
texts.LEB: No rotten word must proceed from your mouth, but only something good for the building up of the need, in order that it may give grace to those who hear,
texts.AMP: Do not let unwholesome [foul, profane, worthless, vulgar] words ever come out of your mouth, but only such speech as is good for building up others, according to the need and the occasion, so that it will be a blessing to those who hear [you speak].
texts.NLT: Don’t use foul or abusive language. Let everything you say be good and helpful, so that your words will be an encouragement to those who hear them.
texts.AKJV: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
texts.NRSV: Let no evil talk come out of your mouths but only what is good for building up, as there is need, so that your words may give grace to those who hear.

texts.NIV84: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, with whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.KJV: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.
texts.NASB95: Do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.NKJV: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.DRB: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God: whereby you are sealed unto the day of redemption.
texts.ESV: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.LEB: and do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.AMP: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God [but seek to please Him], by whom you were sealed and marked [branded as God’s own] for the day of redemption [the final deliverance from the consequences of sin].
texts.NLT: And do not bring sorrow to God’s Holy Spirit by the way you live. Remember, he has identified you as his own, guaranteeing that you will be saved on the day of redemption.
texts.AKJV: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.
texts.NRSV: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, with which you were marked with a seal for the day of redemption.

texts.NIV84: Get rid of all bitterness, rage and anger, brawling and slander, along with every form of malice.
texts.KJV: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
texts.NASB95: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.
texts.NKJV: Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, clamor, and evil speaking be put away from you, with all malice.
texts.DRB: Let all bitterness, and anger, and indignation, and clamour, and blasphemy, be put away from you, with all malice.
texts.ESV: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.
texts.LEB: All bitterness, and rage, and wrath, and clamor, and abusive speech, must be removed from you, together with all wickedness.
texts.AMP: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor [perpetual animosity, resentment, strife, fault-finding] and slander be put away from you, along with every kind of malice [all spitefulness, verbal abuse, malevolence].
texts.NLT: Get rid of all bitterness, rage, anger, harsh words, and slander, as well as all types of evil behavior.
texts.AKJV: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
texts.NRSV: Put away from you all bitterness and wrath and anger and wrangling and slander, together with all malice.

texts.NIV84: Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.
texts.KJV: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
texts.NASB95: Be kind to one another, tender-hearted, forgiving each other, just as God in Christ also has forgiven you.
texts.NKJV: And be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God in Christ forgave you.
texts.DRB: And be ye kind one to another; merciful, forgiving one another, even as God hath forgiven you in Christ.
texts.ESV: Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.
texts.LEB: Become kind toward one another, compassionate, forgiving one another, just as also God in Christ has forgiven you.
texts.AMP: Be kind and helpful to one another, tender-hearted [compassionate, understanding], forgiving one another [readily and freely], just as God in Christ also forgave you.
texts.NLT: Instead, be kind to each other, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, just as God through Christ has forgiven you.
texts.AKJV: and be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath forgiven you.
texts.NRSV: Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ has forgiven you.

texts.NIV84: Wives, submit to your husbands as to the Lord.
texts.KJV: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
texts.NASB95: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord.
texts.NKJV: Wives, submit to your own husbands, as to the Lord.
texts.DRB: Let women be subject to their husbands, as to the Lord:
texts.ESV: Wives, submit to your own husbands, as to the Lord.
texts.LEB: —wives to their own husbands as to the Lord,
texts.AMP: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as [a service] to the Lord.
texts.NLT: For wives, this means submit to your husbands as to the Lord.
texts.AKJV: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
texts.NRSV: Wives, be subject to your husbands as to the Lord,

texts.NIV84: For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior.
texts.KJV: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
texts.NASB95: For the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ also is the head of the church, He Himself being the Savior of the body.
texts.NKJV: For the husband is head of the wife, as also Christ is head of the church; and He is the Savior of the body.
texts.DRB: Because the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ is the head of the church. He is the saviour of his body.
texts.ESV: For the husband is the head of the wife even as Christ is the head of the church, his body, and is himself its Savior.
texts.LEB: because the husband is the head of the wife, as also Christ is the head of the church (he himself being the Savior of the body).
texts.AMP: For the husband is head of the wife, as Christ is head of the church, Himself being the Savior of the body.
texts.NLT: For a husband is the head of his wife as Christ is the head of the church. He is the Savior of his body, the church.
texts.AKJV: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
texts.NRSV: for the husband is the head of the wife just as Christ is the head of the church, his body, and is himself its Savior.

texts.NIV84: Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything.
texts.KJV: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
texts.NASB95: But as the church is subject to Christ, so also the wives ought to be to their husbands in everything.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, just as the church is subject to Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in everything.
texts.DRB: Therefore as the church is subject to Christ, so also let the wives be to their husbands in all things.
texts.ESV: Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit in everything to their husbands.
texts.LEB: But as the church is subject to Christ, thus also wives should be subject to their husbands in everything.
texts.AMP: But as the church is subject to Christ, so also wives should be subject to their husbands in everything [respecting both their position as protector and their responsibility to God as head of the house].
texts.NLT: As the church submits to Christ, so you wives should submit to your husbands in everything.
texts.AKJV: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
texts.NRSV: Just as the church is subject to Christ, so also wives ought to be, in everything, to their husbands.

texts.NIV84: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her
texts.KJV: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
texts.NASB95: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself up for her,
texts.NKJV: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself for her,
texts.DRB: Husbands, love your wives, as Christ also loved the church, and delivered himself up for it:
texts.ESV: Husbands, love your wives, as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her,
texts.LEB: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for her;
texts.AMP: Husbands, love your wives [seek the highest good for her and surround her with a caring, unselfish love], just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself up for her,
texts.NLT: For husbands, this means love your wives, just as Christ loved the church. He gave up his life for her
texts.AKJV: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
texts.NRSV: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her

texts.NIV84: to make her holy, cleansing her by the washing with water through the word,
texts.KJV: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,
texts.NASB95: so that He might sanctify her, having cleansed her by the washing of water with the word,
texts.NKJV: that He might sanctify and cleanse her with the washing of water by the word,
texts.DRB: That he might sanctify it, cleansing it by the laver of water in the word of life:
texts.ESV: that he might sanctify her, having cleansed her by the washing of water with the word,
texts.LEB: in order that he might sanctify her by cleansing her with the washing of water by the word;
texts.AMP: so that He might sanctify the church, having cleansed her by the washing of water with the word [of God],
texts.NLT: to make her holy and clean, washed by the cleansing of God’s word.
texts.AKJV: that he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,
texts.NRSV: in order to make her holy by cleansing her with the washing of water by the word,

texts.NIV84: and to present her to himself as a radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless.
texts.KJV: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
texts.NASB95: that He might present to Himself the church in all her glory, having no spot or wrinkle or any such thing; but that she would be holy and blameless.
texts.NKJV: that He might present her to Himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she should be holy and without blemish.
texts.DRB: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle, or any; such thing; but that it should be holy, and without blemish.
texts.ESV: so that he might present the church to himself in splendor, without spot or wrinkle or any such thing, that she might be holy and without blemish.
texts.LEB: in order that he might present to himself the church glorious, not having a spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she may be holy and blameless.
texts.AMP: so that [in turn] He might present the church to Himself in glorious splendor, without spot or wrinkle or any such thing; but that she would be holy [set apart for God] and blameless.
texts.NLT: He did this to present her to himself as a glorious church without a spot or wrinkle or any other blemish. Instead, she will be holy and without fault.
texts.AKJV: that he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
texts.NRSV: so as to present the church to himself in splendor, without a spot or wrinkle or anything of the kind, so that she may be holy and without blemish.

texts.NIV84: In this same way, husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
texts.KJV: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
texts.NASB95: So husbands ought also to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself;
texts.NKJV: So husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies; he who loves his wife loves himself.
texts.DRB: So also ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife, loveth himself.
texts.ESV: In the same way husbands should love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
texts.LEB: Thus also husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. The one who loves his own wife loves himself.
texts.AMP: Even so husbands should and are morally obligated to love their own wives as [being in a sense] their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself.
texts.NLT: In the same way, husbands ought to love their wives as they love their own bodies. For a man who loves his wife actually shows love for himself.
texts.AKJV: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
texts.NRSV: In the same way, husbands should love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.

texts.NIV84: After all, no one ever hated his own body, but he feeds and cares for it, just as Christ does the church--
texts.KJV: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:
texts.NASB95: for no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as Christ also does the church,
texts.NKJV: For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as the Lord does the church.
texts.DRB: For no man ever hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, as also Christ doth the church:
texts.ESV: For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as Christ does the church,
texts.LEB: For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as also Christ does the church,
texts.AMP: For no one ever hated his own body, but [instead] he nourishes and protects and cherishes it, just as Christ does the church,
texts.NLT: No one hates his own body but feeds and cares for it, just as Christ cares for the church.
texts.AKJV: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:
texts.NRSV: For no one ever hates his own flesh, but he nourishes and tenderly cares for it, just as Christ does for the church,

texts.NIV84: for we are members of his body.
texts.KJV: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
texts.NASB95: because we are members of His body.
texts.NKJV: For we are members of His body, of His flesh and of His bones.
texts.DRB: Because we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
texts.ESV: because we are members of his body.
texts.LEB: because we are members of his body.
texts.AMP: because we are members (parts) of His body.
texts.NLT: And we are members of his body.
texts.AKJV: for we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
texts.NRSV: because we are members of his body.

texts.NIV84: "For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh."
texts.KJV: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
texts.NASB95: For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and shall be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.
texts.NKJV: “For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.”
texts.DRB: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife, and they shall be two in one flesh.
texts.ESV: “Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and hold fast to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.”
texts.LEB: “For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh.”
texts.AMP: FOR THIS REASON A MAN SHALL LEAVE HIS FATHER AND HIS MOTHER AND SHALL BE JOINED [and be faithfully devoted] TO HIS WIFE, AND THE TWO SHALL BECOME ONE FLESH.
texts.NLT: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two are united into one.”
texts.AKJV: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
texts.NRSV: “For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh.”

texts.NIV84: This is a profound mystery--but I am talking about Christ and the church.
texts.KJV: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
texts.NASB95: This mystery is great; but I am speaking with reference to Christ and the church.
texts.NKJV: This is a great mystery, but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
texts.DRB: This is a great sacrament; but I speak in Christ and in the church.
texts.ESV: This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church.
texts.LEB: (This mystery is great, but I am speaking with reference to Christ and the church.)
texts.AMP: This mystery [of two becoming one] is great; but I am speaking with reference to [the relationship of] Christ and the church.
texts.NLT: This is a great mystery, but it is an illustration of the way Christ and the church are one.
texts.AKJV: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
texts.NRSV: This is a great mystery, but I am speaking about Christ and the church.

texts.NIV84: However, each one of you also must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband.
texts.KJV: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.
texts.NASB95: Nevertheless, each individual among you also is to love his own wife even as himself, and the wife must see to it that she respects her husband.
texts.NKJV: Nevertheless let each one of you in particular so love his own wife as himself, and let the wife see that she respects her husband.
texts.DRB: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular love his wife as himself: and let the wife fear her husband.
texts.ESV: However, let each one of you love his wife as himself, and let the wife see that she respects her husband.
texts.LEB: Only you also, each one of you, must thus love his own wife as himself, and the wife must respect her husband.
texts.AMP: However, each man among you [without exception] is to love his wife as his very own self [with behavior worthy of respect and esteem, always seeking the best for her with an attitude of lovingkindness], and the wife [must see to it] that she respects and delights in her husband [that she notices him and prefers him and treats him with loving concern, treasuring him, honoring him, and holding him dear].
texts.NLT: So again I say, each man must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband.
texts.AKJV: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.
texts.NRSV: Each of you, however, should love his wife as himself, and a wife should respect her husband.

blocks.title: The Church is the Body of Christ

texts.NIV84: For this reason, ever since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
texts.KJV: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,
texts.NASB95: For this reason I too, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which exists among you and your love for all the saints,
texts.NKJV: Therefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
texts.DRB: Wherefore I also, hearing of your faith that is in the Lord Jesus, and of your love towards all the saints,
texts.ESV: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love toward all the saints,
texts.LEB: Because of this I also, hearing of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
texts.AMP: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God’s people,
texts.NLT: Ever since I first heard of your strong faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for God’s people everywhere,
texts.AKJV: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,
texts.NRSV: I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love toward all the saints, and for this reason

texts.NIV84: I have not stopped giving thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers.
texts.KJV: Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;
texts.NASB95: do not cease giving thanks for you, while making mention of you in my prayers;
texts.NKJV: do not cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers:
texts.DRB: Cease not to give thanks for you, making commemoration of you in my prayers,
texts.ESV: I do not cease to give thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers,
texts.LEB: do not cease giving thanks for you, making mention in my prayers,
texts.AMP: I do not cease to give thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers;
texts.NLT: I have not stopped thanking God for you. I pray for you constantly,
texts.AKJV: cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;
texts.NRSV: I do not cease to give thanks for you as I remember you in my prayers,

texts.NIV84: I keep asking that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and revelation, so that you may know him better.
texts.KJV: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
texts.NASB95: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of Him.
texts.NKJV: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Him,
texts.DRB: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and of revelation, in the knowledge of him:
texts.ESV: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of him,
texts.LEB: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, may give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him
texts.AMP: [I always pray] that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may grant you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation [that gives you a deep and personal and intimate insight] into the true knowledge of Him [for we know the Father through the Son].
texts.NLT: asking God, the glorious Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, to give you spiritual wisdom and insight so that you might grow in your knowledge of God.
texts.AKJV: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
texts.NRSV: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation as you come to know him,

texts.NIV84: I pray also that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened in order that you may know the hope to which he has called you, the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
texts.KJV: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
texts.NASB95: I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened, so that you will know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
texts.NKJV: the eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that you may know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
texts.DRB: The eyes of your heart enlightened, that you may know what the hope is of the glory of his inheritance in the saints.
texts.ESV: having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
texts.LEB: (the eyes of your hearts having been enlightened), so that you may know what is the hope of his calling, what are the riches of the glory of his inheritance among the saints,
texts.AMP: And [I pray] that the eyes of your heart [the very center and core of your being] may be enlightened [flooded with light by the Holy Spirit], so that you will know and cherish the hope [the divine guarantee, the confident expectation] to which He has called you, the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints (God’s people),
texts.NLT: I pray that your hearts will be flooded with light so that you can understand the confident hope he has given to those he called—his holy people who are his rich and glorious inheritance.
texts.AKJV: the eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
texts.NRSV: so that, with the eyes of your heart enlightened, you may perceive what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance among the saints,

texts.NIV84: and his incomparably great power for us who believe. That power is like the working of his mighty strength,
texts.KJV: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,
texts.NASB95: and what is the surpassing greatness of His power toward us who believe. These are in accordance with the working of the strength of His might
texts.NKJV: and what is the exceeding greatness of His power toward us who believe, according to the working of His mighty power
texts.DRB: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power towards us, who believe according to the operation of the might of his power,
texts.ESV: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his great might
texts.LEB: and what is the surpassing greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his mighty strength
texts.AMP: and [so that you will begin to know] what the immeasurable and unlimited and surpassing greatness of His [active, spiritual] power is in us who believe. These are in accordance with the working of His mighty strength
texts.NLT: I also pray that you will understand the incredible greatness of God’s power for us who believe him. This is the same mighty power
texts.AKJV: and what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,
texts.NRSV: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power for us who believe, according to the working of his great power.

texts.NIV84: which he exerted in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly realms,
texts.KJV: Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NASB95: which He brought about in Christ, when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NKJV: which He worked in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.DRB: Which he wrought in Christ, raising him up from the dead, and setting him on his right hand in the heavenly places.
texts.ESV: that he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.LEB: which he has worked in Christ, raising him from the dead and seating him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.AMP: which He produced in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His own right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NLT: that raised Christ from the dead and seated him in the place of honor at God’s right hand in the heavenly realms.
texts.AKJV: which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NRSV: God put this power to work in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,

texts.NIV84: far above all rule and authority, power and dominion, and every title that can be given, not only in the present age but also in the one to come.
texts.KJV: Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:
texts.NASB95: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
texts.NKJV: far above all principality and power and might and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in that which is to come.
texts.DRB: Above all principality, and power, and virtue, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come.
texts.ESV: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and above every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
texts.LEB: above all rule and authority and power and lordship and every name named, not only in this age but also in the coming one,
texts.AMP: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion [whether angelic or human], and [far above] every name that is named [above every title that can be conferred], not only in this age and world but also in the one to come.
texts.NLT: Now he is far above any ruler or authority or power or leader or anything else—not only in this world but also in the world to come.
texts.AKJV: far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:
texts.NRSV: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion and above every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the age to come.

texts.NIV84: And God placed all things under his feet and appointed him to be head over everything for the church,
texts.KJV: And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,
texts.NASB95: And He put all things in subjection under His feet, and gave Him as head over all things to the church,
texts.NKJV: And He put all things under His feet, and gave Him to be head over all things to the church,
texts.DRB: And he hath subjected all things under his feet, and hath made him head over all the church,
texts.ESV: And he put all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church,
texts.LEB: and he subjected all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church,
texts.AMP: And He put all things [in every realm] in subjection under Christ’s feet, and appointed Him as [supreme and authoritative] head over all things in the church,
texts.NLT: God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of the church.
texts.AKJV: and hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,
texts.NRSV: And he has put all things under his feet and has made him the head over all things for the church,

texts.NIV84: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills everything in every way.
texts.KJV: Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.
texts.NASB95: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.
texts.NKJV: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.
texts.DRB: Which is his body, and the fulness of him who is filled all in all.
texts.ESV: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.
texts.LEB: which is his body, the fullness of the one who fills all things in every way.
texts.AMP: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills and completes all things in all [believers].
texts.NLT: And the church is his body; it is made full and complete by Christ, who fills all things everywhere with himself.
texts.AKJV: which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.
texts.NRSV: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.

texts.NIV84: Now, brothers, about times and dates we do not need to write to you,
texts.KJV: But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you.
texts.NASB95: Now as to the times and the epochs, brethren, you have no need of anything to be written to you.
texts.NKJV: But concerning the times and the seasons, brethren, you have no need that I should write to you.
texts.DRB: But of the times and moments, brethren, you need not, that we should write to you;
texts.ESV: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need to have anything written to you.
texts.LEB: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need of anything to be written to you,
texts.AMP: Now as to the times and dates, brothers and sisters, you have no need for anything to be written to you.
texts.NLT: Now concerning how and when all this will happen, dear brothers and sisters, we don’t really need to write you.
texts.AKJV: But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you.
texts.NRSV: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers and sisters, you do not need to have anything written to you.

texts.NIV84: for you know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
texts.KJV: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
texts.NASB95: For you yourselves know full well that the day of the Lord will come just like a thief in the night.
texts.NKJV: For you yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so comes as a thief in the night.
texts.DRB: For yourselves know perfectly, that the day of the Lord shall so come, as a thief in the night.
texts.ESV: For you yourselves are fully aware that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
texts.LEB: for you yourselves well know that the day of the Lord is coming in the same way as a thief in the night.
texts.AMP: For you yourselves know perfectly well that the day of the [return of the] Lord is coming just as a thief [comes unexpectedly and suddenly] in the night.
texts.NLT: For you know quite well that the day of the Lord’s return will come unexpectedly, like a thief in the night.
texts.AKJV: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
texts.NRSV: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.

texts.NIV84: While people are saying, "Peace and safety," destruction will come on them suddenly, as labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
texts.KJV: For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
texts.NASB95: While they are saying, “Peace and safety!” then destruction will come upon them suddenly like labor pains upon a woman with child, and they will not escape.
texts.NKJV: For when they say, “Peace and safety!” then sudden destruction comes upon them, as labor pains upon a pregnant woman. And they shall not escape.
texts.DRB: For when they shall say, peace and security; then shall sudden destruction come upon them, as the pains upon her that is with child, and they shall not escape.
texts.ESV: While people are saying, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction will come upon them as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
texts.LEB: Whenever they say “Peace and security,” then sudden destruction will overtake them like the birth pains of a pregnant woman, and they will not possibly escape.
texts.AMP: While they are saying, “Peace and safety [all is well and secure!]” then [in a moment unforeseen] destruction will come upon them suddenly like labor pains on a woman with child, and they will absolutely not escape [for there will be no way to escape the judgment of the Lord].
texts.NLT: When people are saying, “Everything is peaceful and secure,” then disaster will fall on them as suddenly as a pregnant woman’s labor pains begin. And there will be no escape.
texts.AKJV: For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
texts.NRSV: When they say, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction will come upon them, as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and there will be no escape!

texts.NIV84: But you, brothers, are not in darkness so that this day should surprise you like a thief.
texts.KJV: But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.NASB95: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, that the day would overtake you like a thief;
texts.NKJV: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, so that this Day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.DRB: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.ESV: But you are not in darkness, brothers, for that day to surprise you like a thief.
texts.LEB: But you, brothers, are not in the darkness, so that the day should catch you like a thief,
texts.AMP: But you, believers, [all you who believe in Christ as Savior and acknowledge Him as God’s Son] are not in spiritual darkness [nor held by its power], that the day [of judgment] would overtake you [by surprise] like a thief;
texts.NLT: But you aren’t in the dark about these things, dear brothers and sisters, and you won’t be surprised when the day of the Lord comes like a thief.
texts.AKJV: But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.NRSV: But you, brothers and sisters, are not in darkness, for that day to surprise you like a thief;

texts.NIV84: You are all sons of the light and sons of the day. We do not belong to the night or to the darkness.
texts.KJV: Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.
texts.NASB95: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We are not of night nor of darkness;
texts.NKJV: You are all sons of light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
texts.DRB: For all you are the children of light, and children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.
texts.ESV: For you are all children of light, children of the day. We are not of the night or of the darkness.
texts.LEB: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
texts.AMP: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We do not belong to the night nor to darkness.
texts.NLT: For you are all children of the light and of the day; we don’t belong to darkness and night.
texts.AKJV: Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.
texts.NRSV: for you are all children of light and children of the day; we are not of the night or of darkness.

texts.NIV84: So then, let us not be like others, who are asleep, but let us be alert and self-controlled.
texts.KJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others; but let us watch and be sober.
texts.NASB95: so then let us not sleep as others do, but let us be alert and sober.
texts.NKJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as others do, but let us watch and be sober.
texts.DRB: Therefore, let us not sleep, as others do; but let us watch, and be sober.
texts.ESV: So then let us not sleep, as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober.
texts.LEB: So then, we must not sleep like the rest, but must be on the alert and be self-controlled.
texts.AMP: So then let us not sleep [in spiritual indifference] as the rest [of the world does], but let us keep wide awake [alert and cautious] and let us be sober [self-controlled, calm, and wise].
texts.NLT: So be on your guard, not asleep like the others. Stay alert and be clearheaded.
texts.AKJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others; but let us watch and be sober.
texts.NRSV: So, then, let us not fall asleep as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober,

texts.NIV84: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.
texts.KJV: For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
texts.NASB95: For those who sleep do their sleeping at night, and those who get drunk get drunk at night.
texts.NKJV: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk are drunk at night.
texts.DRB: For they that sleep, sleep in the night; and they that are drunk, are drunk in the night.
texts.ESV: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, are drunk at night.
texts.LEB: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, are drunk at night.
texts.AMP: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.
texts.NLT: Night is the time when people sleep and drinkers get drunk.
texts.AKJV: For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
texts.NRSV: for those who sleep sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.

texts.NIV84: But since we belong to the day, let us be self-controlled, putting on faith and love as a breastplate, and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
texts.KJV: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation.
texts.NASB95: But since we are of the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet, the hope of salvation.
texts.NKJV: But let us who are of the day be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet the hope of salvation.
texts.DRB: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, having on the breastplate of faith and charity, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.
texts.ESV: But since we belong to the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.
texts.LEB: But because we are of the day, we must be sober, by putting on the breastplate of faith and love and as a helmet the hope of salvation,
texts.AMP: But since we [believers] belong to the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet, the hope and confident assurance of salvation.
texts.NLT: But let us who live in the light be clearheaded, protected by the armor of faith and love, and wearing as our helmet the confidence of our salvation.
texts.AKJV: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation.
texts.NRSV: But since we belong to the day, let us be sober and put on the breastplate of faith and love and for a helmet the hope of salvation.

texts.NIV84: For God did not appoint us to suffer wrath but to receive salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.KJV: For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NASB95: For God has not destined us for wrath, but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NKJV: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.DRB: For God hath not appointed us unto wrath, but unto the purchasing of salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.ESV: For God has not destined us for wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.LEB: because God did not appoint us for wrath, but for the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.AMP: For God has not destined us to [incur His] wrath [that is, He did not select us to condemn us], but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NLT: For God chose to save us through our Lord Jesus Christ, not to pour out his anger on us.
texts.AKJV: For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NRSV: For God has destined us not for wrath but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,

texts.NIV84: He died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we may live together with him.
texts.KJV: Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.
texts.NASB95: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with Him.
texts.NKJV: who died for us, that whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with Him.
texts.DRB: Who died for us; that, whether we watch or sleep, we may live together with him.
texts.ESV: who died for us so that whether we are awake or asleep we might live with him.
texts.LEB: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we will live at the same time with him.
texts.AMP: who died [willingly] for us, so that whether we are awake (alive) or asleep (dead) [at Christ’s appearing], we will live together with Him [sharing eternal life].
texts.NLT: Christ died for us so that, whether we are dead or alive when he returns, we can live with him forever.
texts.AKJV: who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.
texts.NRSV: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep we may live with him.

texts.NIV84: Therefore encourage one another and build each other up, just as in fact you are doing.
texts.KJV: Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do.
texts.NASB95: Therefore encourage one another and build up one another, just as you also are doing.
texts.NKJV: Therefore comfort each other and edify one another, just as you also are doing.
texts.DRB: For which cause comfort one another; and edify one another, as you also do.
texts.ESV: Therefore encourage one another and build one another up, just as you are doing.
texts.LEB: Therefore encourage one another and build up each other, just as indeed you are doing.
texts.AMP: Therefore encourage and comfort one another and build up one another, just as you are doing.
texts.NLT: So encourage each other and build each other up, just as you are already doing.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do.
texts.NRSV: Therefore encourage one another and build up each other, as indeed you are doing.

texts.NIV84: Now we ask you, brothers, to respect those who work hard among you, who are over you in the Lord and who admonish you.
texts.KJV: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;
texts.NASB95: But we request of you, brethren, that you appreciate those who diligently labor among you, and have charge over you in the Lord and give you instruction,
texts.NKJV: And we urge you, brethren, to recognize those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
texts.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them who labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you:
texts.ESV: We ask you, brothers, to respect those who labor among you and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
texts.LEB: Now we ask you, brothers, to respect those who labor among you and rule over you in the Lord and admonish you,
texts.AMP: Now we ask you, brothers and sisters, to appreciate those who diligently work among you [recognize, acknowledge, and respect your leaders], who are in charge over you in the Lord and who give you instruction,
texts.NLT: Dear brothers and sisters, honor those who are your leaders in the Lord’s work. They work hard among you and give you spiritual guidance.
texts.AKJV: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;
texts.NRSV: But we appeal to you, brothers and sisters, to respect those who labor among you and have charge of you in the Lord and admonish you;

texts.NIV84: Hold them in the highest regard in love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
texts.KJV: And to esteem them very highly in love for their work's sake. And be at peace among yourselves.
texts.NASB95: and that you esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Live in peace with one another.
texts.NKJV: and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
texts.DRB: That you esteem them more abundantly in charity, for their work's sake. Have peace with them.
texts.ESV: and to esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
texts.LEB: and to esteem them beyond all measure in love, because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
texts.AMP: and [we ask that you appreciate them and] hold them in the highest esteem in love because of their work [on your behalf]. Live in peace with one another.
texts.NLT: Show them great respect and wholehearted love because of their work. And live peacefully with each other.
texts.AKJV: and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. And be at peace among yourselves.
texts.NRSV: esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.

texts.NIV84: And we urge you, brothers, warn those who are idle, encourage the timid, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
texts.KJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all men.
texts.NASB95: We urge you, brethren, admonish the unruly, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
texts.NKJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn those who are unruly, comfort the fainthearted, uphold the weak, be patient with all.
texts.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, rebuke the unquiet, comfort the feeble minded, support the weak, be patient towards all men.
texts.ESV: And we urge you, brothers, admonish the idle, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with them all.
texts.LEB: And we urge you, brothers, admonish the disorderly, console the discouraged, help the sick, be patient toward all people.
texts.AMP: We [earnestly] urge you, believers, admonish those who are out of line [the undisciplined, the unruly, the disorderly], encourage the timid [who lack spiritual courage], help the [spiritually] weak, be very patient with everyone [always controlling your temper].
texts.NLT: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are lazy. Encourage those who are timid. Take tender care of those who are weak. Be patient with everyone.
texts.AKJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all men.
texts.NRSV: And we urge you, brothers and sisters, to admonish the idlers, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with all of them.

texts.NIV84: Make sure that nobody pays back wrong for wrong, but always try to be kind to each other and to everyone else.
texts.KJV: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
texts.NASB95: See that no one repays another with evil for evil, but always seek after that which is good for one another and for all people.
texts.NKJV: See that no one renders evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good both for yourselves and for all.
texts.DRB: See that none render evil for evil to any man; but ever follow that which is good towards each other, and towards all men.
texts.ESV: See that no one repays anyone evil for evil, but always seek to do good to one another and to everyone.
texts.LEB: See to it that no one pays back evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue good toward one another and toward all people.
texts.AMP: See that no one repays another with evil for evil, but always seek that which is good for one another and for all people.
texts.NLT: See that no one pays back evil for evil, but always try to do good to each other and to all people.
texts.AKJV: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
texts.NRSV: See that none of you repays evil for evil, but always seek to do good to one another and to all.

texts.NIV84: Be joyful always;
texts.KJV: Rejoice evermore.
texts.NASB95: Rejoice always;
texts.NKJV: Rejoice always,
texts.DRB: Always rejoice.
texts.ESV: Rejoice always,
texts.LEB: Rejoice always,
texts.AMP: Rejoice always and delight in your faith;
texts.NLT: Always be joyful.
texts.AKJV: Rejoice evermore.
texts.NRSV: Rejoice always,

texts.NIV84: pray continually;
texts.KJV: Pray without ceasing.
texts.NASB95: pray without ceasing;
texts.NKJV: pray without ceasing,
texts.DRB: Pray without ceasing.
texts.ESV: pray without ceasing,
texts.LEB: pray constantly,
texts.AMP: be unceasing and persistent in prayer;
texts.NLT: Never stop praying.
texts.AKJV: Pray without ceasing.
texts.NRSV: pray without ceasing,

texts.NIV84: give thanks in all circumstances, for this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.KJV: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
texts.NASB95: in everything give thanks; for this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.NKJV: in everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
texts.DRB: In all things give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you all.
texts.ESV: give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
texts.LEB: give thanks in everything; for this is the will of God for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.AMP: in every situation [no matter what the circumstances] be thankful and continually give thanks to God; for this is the will of God for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.NLT: Be thankful in all circumstances, for this is God’s will for you who belong to Christ Jesus.
texts.AKJV: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
texts.NRSV: give thanks in all circumstances, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.

texts.NIV84: Do not put out the Spirit's fire;
texts.KJV: Quench not the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: Do not quench the Spirit;
texts.NKJV: Do not quench the Spirit.
texts.DRB: Extinguish not the spirit.
texts.ESV: Do not quench the Spirit.
texts.LEB: Do not quench the Spirit.
texts.AMP: Do not quench [subdue, or be unresponsive to the working and guidance of] the [Holy] Spirit.
texts.NLT: Do not stifle the Holy Spirit.
texts.AKJV: Quench not the Spirit.
texts.NRSV: Do not quench the Spirit.

texts.NIV84: do not treat prophecies with contempt.
texts.KJV: Despise not prophesyings.
texts.NASB95: do not despise prophetic utterances.
texts.NKJV: Do not despise prophecies.
texts.DRB: Despise not prophecies.
texts.ESV: Do not despise prophecies,
texts.LEB: Do not despise prophecies,
texts.AMP: Do not scorn or reject gifts of prophecy or prophecies [spoken revelations—words of instruction or exhortation or warning].
texts.NLT: Do not scoff at prophecies,
texts.AKJV: Despise not prophesyings.
texts.NRSV: Do not despise prophecies,

texts.NIV84: Test everything. Hold on to the good.
texts.KJV: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
texts.NASB95: But examine everything carefully; hold fast to that which is good;
texts.NKJV: Test all things; hold fast what is good.
