texts.DRB: But prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
texts.ESV: but test everything; hold fast what is good.
texts.LEB: but examine all things; hold fast to what is good.
texts.AMP: But test all things carefully [so you can recognize what is good]. Hold firmly to that which is good.
texts.NLT: but test everything that is said. Hold on to what is good.
texts.AKJV: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
texts.NRSV: but test everything; hold fast to what is good;

texts.NIV84: Avoid every kind of evil.
texts.KJV: Abstain from all appearance of evil.
texts.NASB95: abstain from every form of evil.
texts.NKJV: Abstain from every form of evil.
texts.DRB: From all appearance of evil refrain yourselves.
texts.ESV: Abstain from every form of evil.
texts.LEB: Abstain from every form of evil.
texts.AMP: Abstain from every form of evil [withdraw and keep away from it].
texts.NLT: Stay away from every kind of evil.
texts.AKJV: Abstain from all appearance of evil.
texts.NRSV: abstain from every form of evil.

texts.NIV84: May God himself, the God of peace, sanctify you through and through. May your whole spirit, soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.KJV: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NASB95: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you entirely; and may your spirit and soul and body be preserved complete, without blame at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NKJV: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you completely; and may your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.DRB: And may the God of peace himself sanctify you in all things; that your whole spirit, and soul, and body, may be preserved blameless in the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.ESV: Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.LEB: Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your spirit and soul and body be kept complete, blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.AMP: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you through and through [that is, separate you from profane and vulgar things, make you pure and whole and undamaged—consecrated to Him—set apart for His purpose]; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept complete and [be found] blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NLT: Now may the God of peace make you holy in every way, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ comes again.
texts.AKJV: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NRSV: May the God of peace himself sanctify you entirely, and may your spirit and soul and body be kept sound and blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

texts.NIV84: The one who calls you is faithful and he will do it.
texts.KJV: Faithful is he that calleth you, who also will do it.
texts.NASB95: Faithful is He who calls you, and He also will bring it to pass.
texts.NKJV: He who calls you is faithful, who also will do it.
texts.DRB: He is faithful who hath called you, who also will do it.
texts.ESV: He who calls you is faithful; he will surely do it.
texts.LEB: The one who calls you is faithful, who also will do this.
texts.AMP: Faithful and absolutely trustworthy is He who is calling you [to Himself for your salvation], and He will do it [He will fulfill His call by making you holy, guarding you, watching over you, and protecting you as His own].
texts.NLT: God will make this happen, for he who calls you is faithful.
texts.AKJV: Faithful is he that calleth you, who also will do it.
texts.NRSV: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will do this.

texts.NIV84: Brothers, pray for us.
texts.KJV: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.NASB95: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.NKJV: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.DRB: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.ESV: Brothers, pray for us.
texts.LEB: Brothers, pray for us.
texts.AMP: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
texts.NLT: Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us.
texts.AKJV: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.

texts.NIV84: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
texts.KJV: Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
texts.NASB95: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
texts.NKJV: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
texts.DRB: Salute all the brethren with a holy kiss.
texts.ESV: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
texts.LEB: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
texts.AMP: Greet all the believers with a holy kiss [as brothers and sisters in God’s family].
texts.NLT: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a sacred kiss.
texts.AKJV: Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
texts.NRSV: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.

texts.NIV84: I charge you before the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
texts.KJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
texts.NASB95: I adjure you by the Lord to have this letter read to all the brethren.
texts.NKJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.
texts.DRB: I charge you by the Lord, that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.
texts.ESV: I put you under oath before the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
texts.LEB: I adjure you by the Lord, have this letter read aloud to all the brothers.
texts.AMP: I solemnly charge you by the Lord to have this letter read before all the congregation.
texts.NLT: I command you in the name of the Lord to read this letter to all the brothers and sisters.
texts.AKJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
texts.NRSV: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the brothers and sisters.

texts.NIV84: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
texts.NASB95: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.NKJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
texts.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
texts.ESV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.LEB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.AMP: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.NLT: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.AKJV:
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
The first epistle unto the Thessalonians was written from Athens.
texts.NRSV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

blocks.title: Idol Feasts and the Lord\u2019s Supper

texts.NIV84: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
texts.KJV: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.DRB: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, fly from the service of idols.
texts.ESV: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.LEB: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
texts.AMP: Therefore, my beloved, run [keep far, far away] from [any sort of] idolatry [and that includes loving anything more than God, or participating in anything that leads to sin and enslaves the soul].
texts.NLT: So, my dear friends, flee from the worship of idols.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.NRSV: Therefore, my beloved, flee from the worship of idols.

texts.NIV84: I speak to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
texts.KJV: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
texts.NASB95: I speak as to wise men; you judge what I say.
texts.NKJV: I speak as to wise men; judge for yourselves what I say.
texts.DRB: I speak as to wise men: judge ye yourselves what I say.
texts.ESV: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
texts.LEB: I am speaking as to sensible people; you judge what I am saying.
texts.AMP: I am speaking as to wise and sensible people; judge [carefully and thoughtfully consider] for yourselves what I say.
texts.NLT: You are reasonable people. Decide for yourselves if what I am saying is true.
texts.AKJV: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
texts.NRSV: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.

texts.NIV84: Is not the cup of thanksgiving for which we give thanks a participation in the blood of Christ? And is not the bread that we break a participation in the body of Christ?
texts.KJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
texts.NASB95: Is not the cup of blessing which we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Is not the bread which we break a sharing in the body of Christ?
texts.NKJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
texts.DRB: The chalice of benediction, which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ ? And the bread, which we break, is it not the partaking of the body of the Lord ?
texts.ESV: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
texts.LEB: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
texts.AMP: Is the cup of blessing which we bless [at the Lord’s Supper] not a sharing in the blood of Christ? [Indeed it is.] Is the bread which we break not a sharing in the body of Christ? [Indeed it is.]
texts.NLT: When we bless the cup at the Lord’s Table, aren’t we sharing in the blood of Christ? And when we break the bread, aren’t we sharing in the body of Christ?
texts.AKJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
texts.NRSV: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?

texts.NIV84: Because there is one loaf, we, who are many, are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.
texts.KJV: For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
texts.NASB95: Since there is one bread, we who are many are one body; for we all partake of the one bread.
texts.NKJV: For we, though many, are one bread and one body; for we all partake of that one bread.
texts.DRB: For we, being many, are one bread, one body, all that partake of one bread.
texts.ESV: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
texts.LEB: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all share from the one bread.
texts.AMP: Since there is one bread, we [believers] who are many are [united into] one body; for we all partake of the one bread [which represents the body of Christ].
texts.NLT: And though we are many, we all eat from one loaf of bread, showing that we are one body.
texts.AKJV: For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
texts.NRSV: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.

texts.NIV84: Consider the people of Israel: Do not those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?
texts.KJV: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
texts.NASB95: Look at the nation Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices sharers in the altar?
texts.NKJV: Observe Israel after the flesh: Are not those who eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
texts.DRB: Behold Israel according to the flesh: are not they, that eat of the sacrifices, partakers of the altar ?
texts.ESV: Consider the people of Israel: are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
texts.LEB: Consider Israel according to the flesh: are not the ones who eat the sacrifices sharers in the altar?
texts.AMP: Consider the people of Israel; are those who eat the sacrifices not partners of the altar [united in their worship of the same God]? [Indeed they are.]
texts.NLT: Think about the people of Israel. Weren’t they united by eating the sacrifices at the altar?
texts.AKJV: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
texts.NRSV: Consider the people of Israel: Are not those who eat the sacrifices partners in the altar?

texts.NIV84: Do I mean then that a sacrifice offered to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.KJV: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
texts.NASB95: What do I mean then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.NKJV: What am I saying then? That an idol is anything, or what is offered to idols is anything?
texts.DRB: What then ? Do I say, that what is offered in sacrifice to idols, is any thing ? Or, that the idol is any thing ?
texts.ESV: What do I imply then? That food offered to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.LEB: Therefore, what am I saying? That food sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.AMP: What do I mean then? That a thing offered to idols is anything [special or changed simply because it is offered], or that an idol is anything?
texts.NLT: What am I trying to say? Am I saying that food offered to idols has some significance, or that idols are real gods?
texts.AKJV: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
texts.NRSV: What do I imply, then? That food sacrificed to idols is anything or that an idol is anything?

texts.NIV84: No, but the sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to be participants with demons.
texts.KJV: But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
texts.NASB95: No, but I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God; and I do not want you to become sharers in demons.
texts.NKJV: Rather, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I do not want you to have fellowship with demons.
texts.DRB: But the things which the heathens sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God. And I would not that you should be made partakers with devils.
texts.ESV: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice they offer to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons.
texts.LEB: No, but that the things which they sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I do not want you to become sharers with demons.
texts.AMP: On the contrary, the things which the Gentiles (pagans) sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons [in effect], and not to God; and I do not want you to become partners with demons [by eating at feasts in pagan temples].
texts.NLT: No, not at all. I am saying that these sacrifices are offered to demons, not to God. And I don’t want you to participate with demons.
texts.AKJV: But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
texts.NRSV: No, I imply that what they sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be partners with demons.

texts.NIV84: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons too; you cannot have a part in both the Lord's table and the table of demons.
texts.KJV: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord's table, and of the table of devils.
texts.NASB95: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
texts.NKJV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the Lord’s table and of the table of demons.
texts.DRB: You cannot drink the chalice of the Lord, and the chalice of devils: you cannot be partakers of the table of the Lord, and of the table of devils.
texts.ESV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
texts.LEB: You are not able to drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You are not able to share the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
texts.AMP: You cannot drink [both] the Lord’s cup and the cup of demons. You cannot share in both the Lord’s table and the table of demons [thereby becoming partners with them].
texts.NLT: You cannot drink from the cup of the Lord and from the cup of demons, too. You cannot eat at the Lord’s Table and at the table of demons, too.
texts.AKJV: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
texts.NRSV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.

texts.NIV84: Are we trying to arouse the Lord's jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
texts.KJV: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
texts.NASB95: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? We are not stronger than He, are we?
texts.NKJV: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?
texts.DRB: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy ? Are we stronger than he? All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient.
texts.ESV: Shall we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
texts.LEB: Or are we attempting to provoke the Lord to jealousy? We are not stronger than he is, are we?
texts.AMP: Do we [really] provoke the Lord to jealousy [when we eat food sacrificed to handmade “gods” at pagan feasts]? Are we [spiritually] stronger than He? [Certainly not! He knows that the idols are nothing. But we deeply offend Him.]
texts.NLT: What? Do we dare to rouse the Lord’s jealousy? Do you think we are stronger than he is?
texts.AKJV: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
texts.NRSV: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

texts.NIV84: In the following directives I have no praise for you, for your meetings do more harm than good.
texts.KJV: Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
texts.NASB95: But in giving this instruction, I do not praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
texts.NKJV: Now in giving these instructions I do not praise you, since you come together not for the better but for the worse.
texts.DRB: Now this I ordain: not praising you, that you come together not for the better, but for the worse.
texts.ESV: But in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
texts.LEB: But in giving this instruction I do not praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
texts.AMP: But in giving this next instruction, I do not praise you, because when you meet together it is not for the better but for the worse.
texts.NLT: But in the following instructions, I cannot praise you. For it sounds as if more harm than good is done when you meet together.
texts.AKJV: Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
texts.NRSV: Now in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.

texts.NIV84: In the first place, I hear that when you come together as a church, there are divisions among you, and to some extent I believe it.
texts.KJV: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
texts.NASB95: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that divisions exist among you; and in part I believe it.
texts.NKJV: For first of all, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
texts.DRB: For first of all I hear that when you come together in the church, there are schisms among you; and in part I believe it.
texts.ESV: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you. And I believe it in part,
texts.LEB: For in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
texts.AMP: For, in the first place, when you meet together in church, I hear that there are divisions among you; and in part I believe it,
texts.NLT: First, I hear that there are divisions among you when you meet as a church, and to some extent I believe it.
texts.AKJV: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
texts.NRSV: For, to begin with, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and to some extent I believe it.

texts.NIV84: No doubt there have to be differences among you to show which of you have God's approval.
texts.KJV: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
texts.NASB95: For there must also be factions among you, so that those who are approved may become evident among you.
texts.NKJV: For there must also be factions among you, that those who are approved may be recognized among you.
texts.DRB: For there must be also heresies: that they also, who are approved, may be made manifest among you.
texts.ESV: for there must be factions among you in order that those who are genuine among you may be recognized.
texts.LEB: For indeed it is necessary that there be factions among you, in order that those who are approved may become evident among you.
texts.AMP: for [doubtless] there have to be factions among you, so that those who are of approved character may be clearly recognized among you.
texts.NLT: But, of course, there must be divisions among you so that you who have God’s approval will be recognized!
texts.AKJV: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
texts.NRSV: Indeed, there have to be factions among you, for only so will it become clear who among you are genuine.

texts.NIV84: When you come together, it is not the Lord's Supper you eat,
texts.KJV: When ye come together therefore into one place, this is not to eat the Lord's supper.
texts.NASB95: Therefore when you meet together, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper,
texts.NKJV: Therefore when you come together in one place, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper.
texts.DRB: When you come therefore together into one place, it is not now to eat the Lord's supper.
texts.ESV: When you come together, it is not the Lord's supper that you eat.
texts.LEB: Therefore, when you come together in the same place, it is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
texts.AMP: So when you meet together, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper,
texts.NLT: When you meet together, you are not really interested in the Lord’s Supper.
texts.AKJV: When ye come together therefore into one place, this is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
texts.NRSV: When you come together, it is not really to eat the Lord’s supper.

texts.NIV84: for as you eat, each of you goes ahead without waiting for anybody else. One remains hungry, another gets drunk.
texts.KJV: For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
texts.NASB95: for in your eating each one takes his own supper first; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.NKJV: For in eating, each one takes his own supper ahead of others; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.DRB: For every one taketh before his own supper to eat. And one indeed is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.ESV: For in eating, each one goes ahead with his own meal. One goes hungry, another gets drunk.
texts.LEB: For when you eat it, each one of you goes ahead to take his own supper, and one is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.AMP: for when you eat, each one hurries to get his own supper first [not waiting for others or the poor]. So one goes hungry while another gets drunk.
texts.NLT: For some of you hurry to eat your own meal without sharing with others. As a result, some go hungry while others get drunk.
texts.AKJV: For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
texts.NRSV: For when the time comes to eat, each of you proceeds to eat your own supper, and one goes hungry and another becomes drunk.

texts.NIV84: Don't you have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? Certainly not!
texts.KJV: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? what shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
texts.NASB95: What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I will not praise you.
texts.NKJV: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.
texts.DRB: What, have you not houses to eat and to drink in? Or despise ye the church of God; and put them to shame that have not ? What shall I say to you? Do I praise you? In this I praise you not.
texts.ESV: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I commend you in this? No, I will not.
texts.LEB: For do you not have houses for eating and drinking? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who do not have anything? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I will not praise you!
texts.AMP: What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those [impoverished believers] who have nothing? What will I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? In this I will not praise you!
texts.NLT: What? Don’t you have your own homes for eating and drinking? Or do you really want to disgrace God’s church and shame the poor? What am I supposed to say? Do you want me to praise you? Well, I certainly will not praise you for this!
texts.AKJV: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
texts.NRSV: What! Do you not have households to eat and drink in? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I commend you? In this matter I do not commend you!

texts.NIV84: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: The Lord Jesus, on the night he was betrayed, took bread,
texts.KJV: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, that the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
texts.NASB95: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus in the night in which He was betrayed took bread;
texts.NKJV: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you: that the Lord Jesus on the same night in which He was betrayed took bread;
texts.DRB: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, that the Lord Jesus, the same night in which he was betrayed, took bread.
texts.ESV: For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took bread,
texts.LEB: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you, that the Lord Jesus, on the night in which he was betrayed, took bread,
texts.AMP: For I received from the Lord Himself that [instruction] which I passed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which He was betrayed took bread;
texts.NLT: For I pass on to you what I received from the Lord himself. On the night when he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took some bread
texts.AKJV: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
texts.NRSV: For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took a loaf of bread,

texts.NIV84: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, "This is my body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of me."
texts.KJV: And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
texts.NASB95: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “This is My body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
texts.NKJV: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “Take, eat; this is My body which is broken for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
texts.DRB: And giving thanks, broke, and said: Take ye, and eat: this is my body, which shall be delivered for you: this do for the commemoration of me.
texts.ESV: and when he had given thanks, he broke it, and said, “This is my body, which is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
texts.LEB: and after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body which is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
texts.AMP: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “This is (represents) My body, which is [offered as a sacrifice] for you. Do this in [affectionate] remembrance of Me.”
texts.NLT: and gave thanks to God for it. Then he broke it in pieces and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
texts.AKJV: and when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
texts.NRSV: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”

texts.NIV84: In the same way, after supper he took the cup, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this, whenever you drink it, in remembrance of me."
texts.KJV: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, this cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
texts.NASB95: In the same way He took the cup also after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”
texts.NKJV: In the same manner He also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood. This do, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”
texts.DRB: In like manner also the chalice, after he had supped, saying: This chalice is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as often as you shall drink, for the commemoration of me.
texts.ESV: In the same way also he took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
texts.LEB: Likewise also the cup, after they had eaten, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
texts.AMP: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant [ratified and established] in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in [affectionate] remembrance of Me.”
texts.NLT: In the same way, he took the cup of wine after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant between God and his people—an agreement confirmed with my blood. Do this in remembrance of me as often as you drink it.”
texts.AKJV: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
texts.NRSV: In the same way he took the cup also, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”

texts.NIV84: For whenever you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
texts.KJV: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord's death till he come.
texts.NASB95: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until He comes.
texts.NKJV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death till He comes.
texts.DRB: For as often as you shall eat this bread, and drink the chalice, you shall shew the death of the Lord, until he come.
texts.ESV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
texts.LEB: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
texts.AMP: For every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you are [symbolically] proclaiming [the fact of] the Lord’s death until He comes [again].
texts.NLT: For every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you are announcing the Lord’s death until he comes again.
texts.AKJV: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord’s death till he come.
texts.NRSV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.

texts.NIV84: Therefore, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.KJV: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.NASB95: Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner, shall be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
texts.NKJV: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.DRB: Therefore whosoever shall eat this bread, or drink the chalice of the Lord unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and of the blood of the Lord.
texts.ESV: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty concerning the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.LEB: So then, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
texts.AMP: So then whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in a way that is unworthy [of Him] will be guilty of [profaning and sinning against] the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.NLT: So anyone who eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord unworthily is guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.NRSV: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be answerable for the body and blood of the Lord.

texts.NIV84: A man ought to examine himself before he eats of the bread and drinks of the cup.
texts.KJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
texts.NASB95: But a man must examine himself, and in so doing he is to eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.NKJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.DRB: But let a man prove himself: and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of the chalice.
texts.ESV: Let a person examine himself, then, and so eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.LEB: But let a person examine himself, and in this way let him eat from the bread and let him drink from the cup.
texts.AMP: But a person must [prayerfully] examine himself [and his relationship to Christ], and only when he has done so should he eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.NLT: That is why you should examine yourself before eating the bread and drinking the cup.
texts.AKJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
texts.NRSV: Examine yourselves, and only then eat of the bread and drink of the cup.

texts.NIV84: For anyone who eats and drinks without recognizing the body of the Lord eats and drinks judgment on himself.
texts.KJV: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord's body.
texts.NASB95: For he who eats and drinks, eats and drinks judgment to himself if he does not judge the body rightly.
texts.NKJV: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
texts.DRB: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh judgment to himself, not discerning the body of the Lord.
texts.ESV: For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.
texts.LEB: For the one who eats and drinks, if he does not recognize the body, eats and drinks judgment against himself.
texts.AMP: For anyone who eats and drinks [without solemn reverence and heartfelt gratitude for the sacrifice of Christ], eats and drinks a judgment on himself if he does not recognize the body [of Christ].
texts.NLT: For if you eat the bread or drink the cup without honoring the body of Christ, you are eating and drinking God’s judgment upon yourself.
texts.AKJV: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
texts.NRSV: For all who eat and drink without discerning the body eat and drink judgment against themselves.

texts.NIV84: That is why many among you are weak and sick, and a number of you have fallen asleep.
texts.KJV: For this cause many are weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
texts.NASB95: For this reason many among you are weak and sick, and a number sleep.
texts.NKJV: For this reason many are weak and sick among you, and many sleep.
texts.DRB: Therefore are there many inform and weak among you, and many sleep.
texts.ESV: That is why many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
texts.LEB: Because of this, many are weak and sick among you, and quite a few have died.
texts.AMP: That [careless and unworthy participation] is the reason why many among you are weak and sick, and a number sleep [in death].
texts.NLT: That is why many of you are weak and sick and some have even died.
texts.AKJV: For this cause many are weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
texts.NRSV: For this reason many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.

texts.NIV84: But if we judged ourselves, we would not come under judgment.
texts.KJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
texts.NASB95: But if we judged ourselves rightly, we would not be judged.
texts.NKJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we would not be judged.
texts.DRB: But if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
texts.ESV: But if we judged ourselves truly, we would not be judged.
texts.LEB: But if we were evaluating ourselves, we would not be judged.
texts.AMP: But if we evaluated and judged ourselves honestly [recognizing our shortcomings and correcting our behavior], we would not be judged.
texts.NLT: But if we would examine ourselves, we would not be judged by God in this way.
texts.AKJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
texts.NRSV: But if we judged ourselves, we would not be judged.

texts.NIV84: When we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned with the world.
texts.KJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
texts.NASB95: But when we are judged, we are disciplined by the Lord so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
texts.NKJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
texts.DRB: But whilst we are judged, we are chastised by the Lord, that we be not condemned with this world.
texts.ESV: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
texts.LEB: But if we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined, in order that we will not be condemned with the world.
texts.AMP: But when we [fall short and] are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined [by undergoing His correction] so that we will not be condemned [to eternal punishment] along with the world.
texts.NLT: Yet when we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
texts.AKJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
texts.NRSV: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.

texts.NIV84: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for each other.
texts.KJV: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
texts.NASB95: So then, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
texts.DRB: Wherefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
texts.ESV: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another—
texts.LEB: So then, my brothers, when you come together in order to eat the Lord’s supper, wait for one another.
texts.AMP: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat [the Lord’s Supper], wait for one another [and see to it that no one is left out].
texts.NLT: So, my dear brothers and sisters, when you gather for the Lord’s Supper, wait for each other.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
texts.NRSV: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

texts.NIV84: If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you meet together it may not result in judgment. And when I come I will give further directions.
texts.KJV: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
texts.NASB95: If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that you will not come together for judgment. The remaining matters I will arrange when I come.
texts.NKJV: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest you come together for judgment. And the rest I will set in order when I come.
texts.DRB: If any man be hungry, let him eat at home; that you come not together unto judgment. And the rest I will set in order, when I come.
texts.ESV: if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home—so that when you come together it will not be for judgment. About the other things I will give directions when I come.
texts.LEB: If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest you come together for judgment. And I will give directions about the remaining matters whenever I come.
texts.AMP: If anyone is too hungry [to wait], let him eat at home, so that you will not come together for judgment [on yourselves]. About the remaining matters [of which I was informed], I will take care of them when I come.
texts.NLT: If you are really hungry, eat at home so you won’t bring judgment upon yourselves when you meet together. I’ll give you instructions about the other matters after I arrive.
texts.AKJV: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
texts.NRSV: If you are hungry, eat at home, so that when you come together, it will not be for your condemnation. About the other things I will give instructions when I come.

blocks.title: Unity and Diversity in the Body

texts.NIV84: The body is a unit, though it is made up of many parts; and though all its parts are many, they form one body. So it is with Christ.
texts.KJV: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
texts.NASB95: For even as the body is one and yet has many members, and all the members of the body, though they are many, are one body, so also is Christ.
texts.NKJV: For as the body is one and has many members, but all the members of that one body, being many, are one body, so also is Christ.
texts.DRB: For as the body is one, and hath many members; and all the members of the body, whereas they are many, yet are one body, so also is Christ.
texts.ESV: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
texts.LEB: For just as the body is one and has many members, but all the members of the body, although they are many, are one body, thus also Christ.
texts.AMP: For just as the body is one and yet has many parts, and all the parts, though many, form [only] one body, so it is with Christ.
texts.NLT: The human body has many parts, but the many parts make up one whole body. So it is with the body of Christ.
texts.AKJV: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
texts.NRSV: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.

texts.NIV84: For we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body--whether Jews or Greeks, slave or free--and we were all given the one Spirit to drink.
texts.KJV: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
texts.NASB95: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free, and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
texts.NKJV: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free—and have all been made to drink into one Spirit.
texts.DRB: For in one Spirit were we all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Gentiles, whether bond or free; and in one Spirit we have all been made to drink.
texts.ESV: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and all were made to drink of one Spirit.
texts.LEB: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free persons, and all were made to drink one Spirit.
texts.AMP: For by one [Holy] Spirit we were all baptized into one body, [spiritually transformed—united together] whether Jews or Greeks (Gentiles), slaves or free, and we were all made to drink of one [Holy] Spirit [since the same Holy Spirit fills each life].
texts.NLT: Some of us are Jews, some are Gentiles, some are slaves, and some are free. But we have all been baptized into one body by one Spirit, and we all share the same Spirit.
texts.AKJV: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
texts.NRSV: For in the one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.

texts.NIV84: Now the body is not made up of one part but of many.
texts.KJV: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.NASB95: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.NKJV: For in fact the body is not one member but many.
texts.DRB: For the body also is not one member, but many.
texts.ESV: For the body does not consist of one member but of many.
texts.LEB: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.AMP: For the [human] body does not consist of one part, but of many [limbs and organs].
texts.NLT: Yes, the body has many different parts, not just one part.
texts.AKJV: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.NRSV: Indeed, the body does not consist of one member but of many.

texts.NIV84: If the foot should say, "Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body," it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
texts.KJV: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NASB95: If the foot says, “Because I am not a hand, I am not a part of the body,” it is not for this reason any the less a part of the body.
texts.NKJV: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
texts.DRB: If the foot should say, because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body ?
texts.ESV: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
texts.LEB: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I am not a part of the body,” not because of this is it not a part of the body.
texts.AMP: If the foot says, “Because I am not a hand, I am not a part of the body,” is it not on the contrary still a part of the body?
texts.NLT: If the foot says, “I am not a part of the body because I am not a hand,” that does not make it any less a part of the body.
texts.AKJV: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NRSV: If the foot would say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.

texts.NIV84: And if the ear should say, "Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body," it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
texts.KJV: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NASB95: And if the ear says, “Because I am not an eye, I am not a part of the body,” it is not for this reason any the less a part of the body.
texts.NKJV: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
texts.DRB: And if the ear should say, because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body ?
texts.ESV: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
texts.LEB: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I am not a part of the body, not because of this is it not a part of the body.
texts.AMP: If the ear says, “Because I am not an eye, I am not a part of the body,” is it not on the contrary still a part of the body?
texts.NLT: And if the ear says, “I am not part of the body because I am not an eye,” would that make it any less a part of the body?
texts.AKJV: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NRSV: And if the ear would say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.

texts.NIV84: If the whole body were an eye, where would the sense of hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.KJV: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
texts.NASB95: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.NKJV: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where would be the smelling?
texts.DRB: If the whole body were the eye, where would be the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where would be the smelling?
texts.ESV: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the sense of hearing? If the whole body were an ear, where would be the sense of smell?
texts.LEB: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.AMP: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole [body] were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.NLT: If the whole body were an eye, how would you hear? Or if your whole body were an ear, how would you smell anything?
texts.AKJV: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
texts.NRSV: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?

texts.NIV84: But in fact God has arranged the parts in the body, every one of them, just as he wanted them to be.
texts.KJV: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
texts.NASB95: But now God has placed the members, each one of them, in the body, just as He desired.
texts.NKJV: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body just as He pleased.
texts.DRB: But now God hath set the members every one of them in the body as it hath pleased him.
texts.ESV: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
texts.LEB: But now God has placed the members, each one of them, in the body just as he wanted.
texts.AMP: But now [as things really are], God has placed and arranged the parts in the body, each one of them, just as He willed and saw fit [with the best balance of function].
texts.NLT: But our bodies have many parts, and God has put each part just where he wants it.
texts.AKJV: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
texts.NRSV: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.

texts.NIV84: If they were all one part, where would the body be?
texts.KJV: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
texts.NASB95: If they were all one member, where would the body be?
texts.NKJV: And if they were all one member, where would the body be?
texts.DRB: And if they all were one member, where would be the body?
texts.ESV: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
texts.LEB: And if they all were one member, where would the body be?
texts.AMP: If they all were a single organ, where would [the rest of] the body be?
texts.NLT: How strange a body would be if it had only one part!
texts.AKJV: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
texts.NRSV: If all were a single member, where would the body be?

texts.NIV84: As it is, there are many parts, but one body.
texts.KJV: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
texts.NASB95: But now there are many members, but one body.
texts.NKJV: But now indeed there are many members, yet one body.
texts.DRB: But now there are many members indeed, yet one body.
texts.ESV: As it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
texts.LEB: But now there are many members, but one body.
texts.AMP: But now [as things really are] there are many parts [different limbs and organs], but a single body.
texts.NLT: Yes, there are many parts, but only one body.
texts.AKJV: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
texts.NRSV: As it is, there are many members yet one body.

texts.NIV84: The eye cannot say to the hand, "I don't need you!" And the head cannot say to the feet, "I don't need you!"
texts.KJV: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
texts.NASB95: And the eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you”; or again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.NKJV: And the eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you”; nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.DRB: And the eye cannot say to the hand: I need not thy help; nor again the head to the feet: I have no need of you.
texts.ESV: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.LEB: Now the eye is not able to say to the hand, “I do not have need of you,” or again, the head to the feet, “I do not have need of you.”
texts.AMP: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.NLT: The eye can never say to the hand, “I don’t need you.” The head can’t say to the feet, “I don’t need you.”
texts.AKJV: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
texts.NRSV: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”

texts.NIV84: On the contrary, those parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
texts.KJV: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
texts.NASB95: On the contrary, it is much truer that the members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary;
texts.NKJV: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.
texts.DRB: Yea, much more those that seem to be the more feeble members of the body, are more necessary.
texts.ESV: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
texts.LEB: But by much more the members of the body which are thought to be weaker are necessary,
texts.AMP: But quite the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are [absolutely] necessary;
texts.NLT: In fact, some parts of the body that seem weakest and least important are actually the most necessary.
texts.AKJV: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
texts.NRSV: On the contrary, the members of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,

texts.NIV84: and the parts that we think are less honorable we treat with special honor. And the parts that are unpresentable are treated with special modesty,
texts.KJV: And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
texts.NASB95: and those members of the body which we deem less honorable, on these we bestow more abundant honor, and our less presentable members become much more presentable,
texts.NKJV: And those members of the body which we think to be less honorable, on these we bestow greater honor; and our unpresentable parts have greater modesty,
texts.DRB: And such as we think to be the less honourable members of the body, about these we put more abundant honour; and those that are our uncomely parts, have more abundant comeliness.
texts.ESV: and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor, and our unpresentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
texts.LEB: and the parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, these we clothe with more abundant honor, and our unpresentable parts come to have more abundant presentability,
texts.AMP: and as for those parts of the body which we consider less honorable, these we treat with greater honor; and our less presentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
texts.NLT: And the parts we regard as less honorable are those we clothe with the greatest care. So we carefully protect those parts that should not be seen,
texts.AKJV: and those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
texts.NRSV: and those members of the body that we think less honorable we clothe with greater honor, and our less respectable members are treated with greater respect,

texts.NIV84: while our presentable parts need no special treatment. But God has combined the members of the body and has given greater honor to the parts that lacked it,
texts.KJV: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked.
texts.NASB95: whereas our more presentable members have no need of it. But God has so composed the body, giving more abundant honor to that member which lacked,
texts.NKJV: but our presentable parts have no need. But God composed the body, having given greater honor to that part which lacks it,
texts.DRB: But our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, giving to that which wanted the more abundant honour,
texts.ESV: which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body, giving greater honor to the part that lacked it,
texts.LEB: but our presentable parts do not have need of this. Yet God composed the body by giving more abundant honor to the part which lacked it,
texts.AMP: while our more presentable parts do not require it. But God has combined the [whole] body, giving greater honor to that part which lacks it,
texts.NLT: while the more honorable parts do not require this special care. So God has put the body together such that extra honor and care are given to those parts that have less dignity.
texts.AKJV: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
texts.NRSV: whereas our more respectable members do not need this. But God has so arranged the body, giving the greater honor to the inferior member,

texts.NIV84: so that there should be no division in the body, but that its parts should have equal concern for each other.
texts.KJV: That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
texts.NASB95: so that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
texts.NKJV: that there should be no schism in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.
texts.DRB: That there might be no schism in the body; but the members might be mutually careful one for another.
texts.ESV: that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
texts.LEB: in order that there not be a division in the body, but the members would have the same concern for one another.
texts.AMP: so that there would be no division or discord in the body [that is, lack of adaptation of the parts to each other], but that the parts may have the same concern for one another.
texts.NLT: This makes for harmony among the members, so that all the members care for each other.
texts.AKJV: that there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
texts.NRSV: that there may be no dissension within the body, but the members may have the same care for one another.

texts.NIV84: If one part suffers, every part suffers with it; if one part is honored, every part rejoices with it.
texts.KJV: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.NASB95: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.NKJV: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; or if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.DRB: And if one member suffer any thing, all the members suffer with it; or if one member glory, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.ESV: If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
texts.LEB: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer together; if a member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.AMP: And if one member suffers, all the parts share the suffering; if one member is honored, all rejoice with it.
texts.NLT: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it, and if one part is honored, all the parts are glad.
texts.AKJV: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.NRSV: If one member suffers, all suffer together with it; if one member is honored, all rejoice together with it.

texts.NIV84: Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it.
texts.KJV: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
texts.NASB95: Now you are Christ’s body, and individually members of it.
texts.NKJV: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
texts.DRB: Now you are the body of Christ, and members of member.
texts.ESV: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
texts.LEB: Now you are the body of Christ, and members of it individually,
texts.AMP: Now you [collectively] are Christ’s body, and individually [you are] members of it [each with his own special purpose and function].
texts.NLT: All of you together are Christ’s body, and each of you is a part of it.
texts.AKJV: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
texts.NRSV: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.

texts.NIV84: And in the church God has appointed first of all apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then workers of miracles, also those having gifts of healing, those able to help others, those with gifts of administration, and those speaking in different kinds of tongues.
texts.KJV: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
texts.NASB95: And God has appointed in the church, first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, various kinds of tongues.
texts.NKJV: And God has appointed these in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, varieties of tongues.
texts.DRB: And God indeed hath set some in the church; first apostles, secondly prophets, thirdly doctors; after that miracles; then the graces of healing, helps, governments, kinds of tongues, interpretations of speeches.
texts.ESV: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helping, administrating, and various kinds of tongues.
texts.LEB: and whom God has appointed in the church: first, apostles, second, prophets, third, teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helps, administrations, kinds of tongues.
texts.AMP: So God has appointed and placed in the church [for His own use]: first apostles [chosen by Christ], second prophets [those who foretell the future, those who speak a new message from God to the people], third teachers, then those who work miracles, then those with the gifts of healings, the helpers, the administrators, and speakers in various kinds of [unknown] tongues.
texts.NLT:
Here are some of the parts God has appointed for the church:
first are apostles,
second are prophets,
third are teachers,
then those who do miracles,
those who have the gift of healing,
those who can help others,
those who have the gift of leadership,
those who speak in unknown languages.
texts.AKJV: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
texts.NRSV: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then deeds of power, then gifts of healing, forms of assistance, forms of leadership, various kinds of tongues.

texts.NIV84: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
texts.KJV: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
texts.NASB95: All are not apostles, are they? All are not prophets, are they? All are not teachers, are they? All are not workers of miracles, are they?
texts.NKJV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
texts.DRB: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all doctors?
texts.ESV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
texts.LEB: Not all are apostles, are they? Not all are prophets, are they? Not all are teachers, are they? Not all are workers of miracles, are they?
texts.AMP: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
texts.NLT: Are we all apostles? Are we all prophets? Are we all teachers? Do we all have the power to do miracles?
texts.AKJV: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
texts.NRSV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work powerful deeds?

texts.NIV84: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.KJV: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
texts.NASB95: All do not have gifts of healings, do they? All do not speak with tongues, do they? All do not interpret, do they?
texts.NKJV: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.DRB: Are all workers of miracles? Have all the grace of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.ESV: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.LEB: Not all have gifts of healing, do they? Not all speak with tongues, do they? Not all interpret, do they?
texts.AMP: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.NLT: Do we all have the gift of healing? Do we all have the ability to speak in unknown languages? Do we all have the ability to interpret unknown languages? Of course not!
texts.AKJV: have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
texts.NRSV: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?

texts.NIV84: But eagerly desire the greater gifts. And now I will show you the most excellent way.
texts.KJV: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
texts.NASB95:
But earnestly desire the greater gifts.
And I show you a still more excellent way.
texts.NKJV: But earnestly desire the best gifts. And yet I show you a more excellent way.
texts.DRB: But be zealous for the better gifts. And I shew unto you yet a more excellent way.
texts.ESV:
But earnestly desire the higher gifts.
And I will show you a still more excellent way.
texts.LEB: But strive for the greater gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.
texts.AMP: But earnestly desire and strive for the greater gifts [if acquiring them is going to be your goal]. And yet I will show you a still more excellent way [one of the choicest graces and the highest of them all: unselfish love].
texts.NLT:
So you should earnestly desire the most helpful gifts.
But now let me show you a way of life that is best of all.
texts.AKJV: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
texts.NRSV: But strive for the greater gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.

texts.NIV84: Now we know that if the earthly tent we live in is destroyed, we have a building from God, an eternal house in heaven, not built by human hands.
texts.KJV: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NASB95: For we know that if the earthly tent which is our house is torn down, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NKJV: For we know that if our earthly house, this tent, is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.DRB: For we know, if our earthly house of this habitation be dissolved, that we have a building of God, a house not made with hands, eternal in heaven.
texts.ESV: For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.LEB: For we know that if our earthly house, the tent, is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made by hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.AMP: For we know that if the earthly tent [our physical body] which is our house is torn down [through death], we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NLT: For we know that when this earthly tent we live in is taken down (that is, when we die and leave this earthly body), we will have a house in heaven, an eternal body made for us by God himself and not by human hands.
texts.AKJV: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NRSV: For we know that, if the earthly tent we live in is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.

texts.NIV84: Meanwhile we groan, longing to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling,
texts.KJV: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
texts.NASB95: For indeed in this house we groan, longing to be clothed with our dwelling from heaven,
texts.NKJV: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven,
texts.DRB: For in this also we groan, desiring to be clothed upon with our habitation that is from heaven.
texts.ESV: For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling,
texts.LEB: For indeed, in this house we groan, because we desire to put on our dwelling from heaven,
texts.AMP: For indeed in this house we groan, longing to be clothed with our [immortal, eternal] celestial dwelling,
texts.NLT: We grow weary in our present bodies, and we long to put on our heavenly bodies like new clothing.
texts.AKJV: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
texts.NRSV: For in this tent we groan, longing to be further clothed with our heavenly dwelling,

texts.NIV84: because when we are clothed, we will not be found naked.
texts.KJV: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
texts.NASB95: inasmuch as we, having put it on, will not be found naked.
texts.NKJV: if indeed, having been clothed, we shall not be found naked.
texts.DRB: Yet so that we be found clothed, not naked.
texts.ESV: if indeed by putting it on we may not be found naked.
texts.LEB: if indeed, even after we have taken it off, we will not be found naked.
texts.AMP: so that by putting it on we will not be found naked.
texts.NLT: For we will put on heavenly bodies; we will not be spirits without bodies.
texts.AKJV: if so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
texts.NRSV: for surely when we have been clothed in it we will not be found naked.

texts.NIV84: For while we are in this tent, we groan and are burdened, because we do not wish to be unclothed but to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
texts.KJV: For we that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
texts.NASB95: For indeed while we are in this tent, we groan, being burdened, because we do not want to be unclothed but to be clothed, so that what is mortal will be swallowed up by life.
texts.NKJV: For we who are in this tent groan, being burdened, not because we want to be unclothed, but further clothed, that mortality may be swallowed up by life.
texts.DRB: For we also, who are in this tabernacle, do groan, being burthened; because we would not be unclothed, but clothed upon, that that which is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
texts.ESV: For while we are still in this tent, we groan, being burdened—not that we would be unclothed, but that we would be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
texts.LEB: For indeed we who are in this tent groan, being burdened for this reason, that we do not want to be unclothed, but to be clothed, in order that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
texts.AMP: For while we are in this tent, we groan, being burdened [often weighed down, oppressed], not that we want to be unclothed [separated by death from the body], but to be clothed, so that what is mortal [the body] will be swallowed up by life [after the resurrection].
texts.NLT: While we live in these earthly bodies, we groan and sigh, but it’s not that we want to die and get rid of these bodies that clothe us. Rather, we want to put on our new bodies so that these dying bodies will be swallowed up by life.
texts.AKJV: For we that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
texts.NRSV: For while we are in this tent, we groan under our burden because we wish not to be unclothed but to be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.

texts.NIV84: Now it is God who has made us for this very purpose and has given us the Spirit as a deposit, guaranteeing what is to come.
texts.KJV: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: Now He who prepared us for this very purpose is God, who gave to us the Spirit as a pledge.
texts.NKJV: Now He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who also has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
texts.DRB: Now he that maketh us for this very thing, is God, who hath given us the pledge of the Spirit.
texts.ESV: He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
texts.LEB: Now the one who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the down payment, the Spirit.
texts.AMP: Now He who has made us and prepared us for this very purpose is God, who gave us the [Holy] Spirit as a pledge [a guarantee, a down payment on the fulfillment of His promise].
texts.NLT: God himself has prepared us for this, and as a guarantee he has given us his Holy Spirit.
texts.AKJV: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
texts.NRSV: The one who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a down payment.

texts.NIV84: Therefore we are always confident and know that as long as we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord.
texts.KJV: Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
texts.NASB95: Therefore, being always of good courage, and knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord—
texts.NKJV: So we are always confident, knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord.
texts.DRB: Therefore having always confidence, knowing that, while we are in the body, we are absent from the Lord.
texts.ESV: So we are always of good courage. We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord,
texts.LEB: Therefore, although we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord—
texts.AMP: So then, being always filled with good courage and confident hope, and knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord—
