texts.NLT: So we are always confident, even though we know that as long as we live in these bodies we are not at home with the Lord.
texts.AKJV: Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
texts.NRSV: So we are always confident, even though we know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord—

texts.NIV84: We live by faith, not by sight.
texts.KJV: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
texts.NASB95: for we walk by faith, not by sight—
texts.NKJV: For we walk by faith, not by sight.
texts.DRB: (For we walk by faith, and not by sight.)
texts.ESV: for we walk by faith, not by sight.
texts.LEB: for we live by faith, not by sight—
texts.AMP: for we walk by faith, not by sight [living our lives in a manner consistent with our confident belief in God’s promises]—
texts.NLT: For we live by believing and not by seeing.
texts.AKJV: (for we walk by faith, not by sight:)
texts.NRSV: for we walk by faith, not by sight.

texts.NIV84: We are confident, I say, and would prefer to be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
texts.KJV: We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
texts.NASB95: we are of good courage, I say, and prefer rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.
texts.NKJV: We are confident, yes, well pleased rather to be absent from the body and to be present with the Lord.
texts.DRB: But we are confident, and have a good will to be absent rather from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
texts.ESV: Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
texts.LEB: so we are confident and prefer rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.
texts.AMP: we are [as I was saying] of good courage and confident hope, and prefer rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.
texts.NLT: Yes, we are fully confident, and we would rather be away from these earthly bodies, for then we will be at home with the Lord.
texts.AKJV: we are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
texts.NRSV: Yes, we do have confidence, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.

texts.NIV84: So we make it our goal to please him, whether we are at home in the body or away from it.
texts.KJV: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
texts.NASB95: Therefore we also have as our ambition, whether at home or absent, to be pleasing to Him.
texts.NKJV: Therefore we make it our aim, whether present or absent, to be well pleasing to Him.
texts.DRB: And therefore we labour, whether absent or present, to please him.
texts.ESV: So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please him.
texts.LEB: Therefore indeed we have as our ambition, whether at home in the body or absent from the body, to be acceptable to him.
texts.AMP: Therefore, whether we are at home [on earth] or away from home [and with Him], it is our [constant] ambition to be pleasing to Him.
texts.NLT: So whether we are here in this body or away from this body, our goal is to please him.
texts.AKJV: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
texts.NRSV: So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to be pleasing to him.

texts.NIV84: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, that each one may receive what is due him for the things done while in the body, whether good or bad.
texts.KJV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
texts.NASB95: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may be recompensed for his deeds in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
texts.NKJV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, that each one may receive the things done in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
texts.DRB: For we must all be manifested before the judgement seat of Christ, that every one may receive the proper things of the body, according as he hath done, whether it be good or evil.
texts.ESV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may receive what is due for what he has done in the body, whether good or evil.
texts.LEB: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, in order that each one may receive back the things through the body according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
texts.AMP: For we [believers will be called to account and] must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may be repaid for what has been done in the body, whether good or bad [that is, each will be held responsible for his actions, purposes, goals, motives—the use or misuse of his time, opportunities and abilities].
texts.NLT: For we must all stand before Christ to be judged. We will each receive whatever we deserve for the good or evil we have done in this earthly body.
texts.AKJV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
texts.NRSV: For all of us must appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each may receive due recompense for actions done in the body, whether good or evil.
